addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1 # FPGA-Imaging-Library. 2 **An open source library for image processing on FPGA**. 3 **一个开源的FPGA图像处理库。** 4 5 *** 6 7 ## Progress 8 Version 1.0 published: 9 1.0版本已发布： 10 [http://fil.dtysky.moe/](http://fil.dtysky.moe) 11
12 系列教程： 13 [FPGA的图像处理](http://dtysky.moe/tag/fpga-imaging-library.html) 14 15 *** 16 17 ## What is FPGA-Imaging-Library? 18 F-I-L is a open source library for image processing on FPGA, it already contents many useful operations, and is updating. All the operations are packaged to IPCores, and of course they follow a same and standardized interface, moreover, each of them can work on piplines-mode and req-ack mode. 19 F-I-L是一个FPGA平台的开源的图像处理库，已经拥有了许多常用操作，并在不断更新中。这些操作被以IP核的形式进行了封装，遵循同一种规范化的接口，同时具有流水线和请求响应两种使用模式。 20 21 *** 22 23 ## How to use? 24 Certainly, all IPCores have their software simulations, functional simulations and testing on board, the same folder structure and interface can make user do simulations and testing conveniently. 25 IPCores just support xilinx vivado now. 26 大部分IP都拥有自己的软件仿真、功能仿真和板上测试，并且具有相同的文件结构和接口，能够让使用者很方便得进行测试和仿真。 27 目前IP形式只支持Xilinx的Vivado套件，不排除未来会向Quartus搬移的可能。 28 29 *** 30 31 ## Author 32 **Tianyu Dai (dtysky)** 33 **戴天宇 (dtysky)** 34 [http://dtysky.moe](http://dtysky.moe) 35 [dtysky@outlook.com](dtysky@outlook.com) 36 [http://proj.dtysky.moe/](http://proj.dtysky.moe/) 37 38 *** 39 40 ## Copyright 41 **Copyright © 2015, 戴天宇, Tianyu Dai (dtysky). All Rights Reserved. 42 This project is free software and released under the** [GNU Lesser General Public License (LGPL)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/lgpl-2.1.html).

file.content:
# FPGA-Imaging-Library. **An open source library for image processing on FPGA**. **一个开源的FPGA图像处理库。** *** ## Progress Version 1.0 published: 1.0版本已发布： [http://fil.dtysky.moe/](http://fil.dtysky.moe)
系列教程： [FPGA的图像处理](http://dtysky.moe/tag/fpga-imaging-library.html) *** ## What is FPGA-Imaging-Library? F-I-L is a open source library for image processing on FPGA, it already contents many useful operations, and is updating. All the operations are packaged to IPCores, and of course they follow a same and standardized interface, moreover, each of them can work on piplines-mode and req-ack mode. F-I-L是一个FPGA平台的开源的图像处理库，已经拥有了许多常用操作，并在不断更新中。这些操作被以IP核的形式进行了封装，遵循同一种规范化的接口，同时具有流水线和请求响应两种使用模式。 *** ## How to use? Certainly, all IPCores have their software simulations, functional simulations and testing on board, the same folder structure and interface can make user do simulations and testing conveniently. IPCores just support xilinx vivado now. 大部分IP都拥有自己的软件仿真、功能仿真和板上测试，并且具有相同的文件结构和接口，能够让使用者很方便得进行测试和仿真。 目前IP形式只支持Xilinx的Vivado套件，不排除未来会向Quartus搬移的可能。 *** ## Author **Tianyu Dai (dtysky)** **戴天宇 (dtysky)** [http://dtysky.moe](http://dtysky.moe) [dtysky@outlook.com](dtysky@outlook.com) [http://proj.dtysky.moe/](http://proj.dtysky.moe/) *** ## Copyright **Copyright © 2015, 戴天宇, Tianyu Dai (dtysky). All Rights Reserved. This project is free software and released under the** [GNU Lesser General Public License (LGPL)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/lgpl-2.1.html).

file.content:
All projects here are used for testing on board for each category, please "*.xpr" with Vivado, then:  

0. Open vivado
1. Cd to your proj_path in tcl console  
example: cd B:/Complex_Mind/FPGA-Imaging-Library/PLv1.0/TestOnBoard/Geometry  
2. Tcl Console: source bulid.tcl   
3. Export Hardware
4. Lanch SDK
5. Create new application project  
6. copy codes in ForBuild/Main.c to your Main.c
7. Change setting and run

content.thinking:
This is an FPGA image processing library written in Verilog/SystemVerilog. The structure is very consistent. Let me understand the overall architecture better. Each IP core has a consistent structure:
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/sources_1/new/<Name>.v` - the main Verilog source
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/sim_1/new/<Name>_TB.sv` - testbench
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/component.xml` - IP packaging
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/xgui/<Name>_v1_0.tcl` - GUI config
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/gui/<Name>_v1_0.gtcl` - GUI generation
- `SoftwareSim/sim.py` - software simulation reference
- `HDLSimDataGen/create.py` - test data generation
- `SimResCheck/compare.py`, `convert.py` - result checking
- `README.md` - documentation
- `ImageForTest/conf.json` - test config

Categories:
- BoardInit_AXI - board initialization
- Connector - data connectors (mux, datawidth convert, etc.)
- Generator - frame/row/window generators
- Geometry - geometric transforms (Crop, Mirror, Pan, Rotate, Scale, Shear)
- InOut - input/output
- LocalFilter - local filters (ErosionDilation, MatchTemplate, MeanFilter, RankFilter, ThresholdLocal)
- Point - point operations (ColorReversal, ContrastTransform, Graying, LightnessTransform, Threshold)
- TestOnBoard - on-board testing projects

Let me look at the full file list to understand the complete set of modules, then dive into key files.

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete picture of the project structure. Let me organize the categories:

**Categories (top-level):**
1. **BoardInit_AXI** - AXI-based board initialization IP (has C drivers, AXI interface)
2. **Connector** - data routing/utilities:
   - ColorBin2Channels, ColorGray2Channels, ColorRGB16toRGB24, ColorRGB24toVGA
   - DataCombin2, DataCombin3, DataDelay, DataSplit4, DataWidthConvert
   - Mux2, Mux4, Mux8, Or8
3. **Generator** - frame/row/window/count generators:
   - CountGenerator, FrameController, FrameController2, RowsGenerator, WindowGenerator
4. **Geometry** - geometric transforms:
   - Crop, Mirror, Pan, Rotate, Scale, Shear
5. **InOut** - input/output hardware:
   - Bram8x320x240, Cam, IIC_Ctrl, VGA640x480
6. **LocalFilter** - neighborhood filters:
   - ErosionDilationBin, MatchTemplateBin, MeanFilter, RankFilter, ThresholdLocal
7. **Point** - point operations:
   - ColorReversal, ContrastTransform, Graying, LightnessTransform, Threshold
8. **TestOnBoard** - on-board test projects (Geometry, LocalFilter, Point)

Each processing IP (in Generator, Geometry, LocalFilter, Point) follows a very consistent folder structure:
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/sources_1/new/<Name>.v` - main Verilog source
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/sim_1/new/<Name>_TB.sv` - SystemVerilog testbench
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/component.xml` - Vivado IP packaging
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/xgui/<Name>_v1_0.tcl` - XGUI parameter config
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/gui/<Name>_v1_0.gtcl` - GUI generation script
- `SoftwareSim/sim.py` - software reference simulation (and sometimes named modules like RowsGenerator.py, WindowGenerator.py, MeanFilter.py)
- `HDLSimDataGen/create.py` - generate test data for HDL sim
- `SimResCheck/compare.py`, `convert.py` - compare sim result vs software result
- `ImageForTest/conf.json` - test image config
- `README.md` - module doc

Now I need to understand the core concepts:
1. The standardized interface (req-ack mode and pipeline mode)
2. The data formats (Gray, Binary, RGB)
3. The simulation methodology (software sim → HDL sim → compare)
4. FrameController / RowsGenerator / WindowGenerator as the core "data feeding" mechanisms

Let me read the key Verilog files to understand the standardized interface. Let me look at:
- A Point operation (simplest): ColorReversal.v, Threshold.v
- A simple one to understand the interface
- FrameController.v and RowsGenerator.v and WindowGenerator.v (core generators)
- The READMEs for some modules
- One of the create.py / sim.py / compare.py to understand the methodology

Let me read several key files in parallel. I'll delegate the broad reading to agents to be efficient, but since I need precise source_files paths and understanding, let me read key files directly.

Let me read the most foundational files. I'll batch reads.

//Com2DocHDL
/*
:Project
FPGA-Imaging-Library

:Design
ContrastTransform

:Function
Get a reversal all ponit's color. 
Give the first output after 1 cycle while the input enable.

:Module
Main module

:Version
1.0

:Modified
2015-05-16

Copyright (C) 2015 Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>

This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
Lesser General Public License for more details.

You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA

Homepage for this project:
	http://fil.dtysky.moe

Sources for this project:
	https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library

My e-mail:
	dtysky@outlook.com

My blog:
	http://dtysky.moe

*/

`timescale 1ns / 1ps

module ColorReversal(
	clk,
	rst_n,
	in_enable,
	in_data,
	out_ready,
	out_data);

	/*
	::description
	This module's working mode.
	::range
	0 for Pipelines, 1 for Req-ack
	*/
	parameter[0 : 0] work_mode = 0; 
	/*
	::description
	Channels for color, 1 for gray, 3 for rgb, etc.
	*/
	parameter color_channels = 3;
	/*
	::description
	Color's bit wide
	::range
	1 - 12
	*/
	parameter[3: 0] color_width = 8;
	/*
	::description
	Clock.
	*/
	input clk;
	/*
	::description
	Reset, active low.
	*/
	input rst_n;
	/*
	::description
	Input data enable, in pipelines mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be changes.
	*/
	input in_enable;
	/*
	::description
	Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
	*/
	input [color_channels * color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
	/*
	::description
	Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
	*/
	output out_ready;
	/*
	::description
	Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
	*/
	output[color_channels * color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;

	reg reg_out_ready;
	reg[color_channels * color_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data;

	genvar i;
	generate

		always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
			if(~rst_n | ~in_enable)
				reg_out_ready <= 0;
			else
				reg_out_ready <= 1;
		end
		assign out_ready = reg_out_ready;

		if(work_mode == 0) begin
			always @(posedge clk)
				reg_out_data <= ~in_data;
		end else begin
			always @(posedge in_enable)
				reg_out_data <= ~in_data;
		end
		assign out_data = out_ready == 0 ? 0 : reg_out_data;

	endgenerate

endmodule

1	# Wiki and Contact
2	[Homepage for this project](http://ifl.dtysky.moe)  
3	[Sources for this project](https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library)  
4	[My e-mail](Mailto:dtysky@outlook.com)  
5	[My blog](http://dtysky.moe)  
6	
7	# Simulations
8	**Simulations for this module just support RGB, Gray-scale and binary images !**  
9	
10	## Steps for simulations
11	**Warning: procedures for simulation depend on python 2.7 and PIL(Python Imaging Library), please get them by your self.**  
12	
13	### Preparing
14	Open "ImageForTest".  
15	Put images which you want to use for simulations.  
16	Edit the "conf.json" for setting the configurations for simulations.  
17	
18	### Software simulation
19	Open "SoftwareSim".  
20	Run "sim.py".  
21	Open "SimResCheck", check your results.  
22	
23	### Creat preparing data for functional simulation
24	Open "HDLSimDataGen".  
25	Run "creat.py".  
26	
27	### Functional simulation
28	Functional simulation just support for modelsim 10.1 up.   
29	Above all, you must compile all xilinx vivado library to modelsim.  
30	
31	Open "FunSimForHDL".  
32	Open "ContrastTranslate.mpf" with modelsim.  
33	
34	#### Do these:
35	
36	Run this commond at first time:
37	
38	    vlib work  
39	
40	Then, compile all your source file:  
41	
42	    modelsim gui -> Compile -> Compile all  
43	
44	Then, run this:
45	
46	    do Run.do
47	
48	If you just want to the finally result, and don't want to watch the waveform, run this:  
49	
50	    do RunOver.do
51	
52	## Comparing
53	Open "SimResCheck".  
54	Run "covert.py", then you can get the results form functional simulation.  
55	Run "compare.py" for creating a report for software simulation and functional simulation.  
56

file.content:
# Wiki and Contact
[Homepage for this project](http://ifl.dtysky.moe)  
[Sources for this project](https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library)  
[My e-mail](Mailto:dtysky@outlook.com)  
[My blog](http://dtysky.moe)  

# Simulations
**Simulations for this module just support RGB, Gray-scale and binary images !**  

## Steps for simulations
**Warning: procedures for simulation depend on python 2.7 and PIL(Python Imaging Library), please get them by your self.**  

### Preparing
Open "ImageForTest".  
Put images which you want to use for simulations.  
Edit the "conf.json" for setting the configurations for simulations.  

### Software simulation
Open "SoftwareSim".  
Run "sim.py".  
Open "SimResCheck", check your results.  

### Creat preparing data for functional simulation
Open "HDLSimDataGen".  
Run "creat.py".  

### Functional simulation
Functional simulation just support for modelsim 10.1 up.   
Above all, you must compile all xilinx vivado library to modelsim.  

Open "FunSimForHDL".  
Open "ContrastTranslate.mpf" with modelsim.  

#### Do these:

Run this commond at first time:

    vlib work  

Then, compile all your source file:  

    modelsim gui -> Compile -> Compile all  

Then, run this:

    do Run.do

If you just want to the finally result, and don't want to watch the waveform, run this:  

    do RunOver.do

## Comparing
Open "SimResCheck".  
Run "covert.py", then you can get the results form functional simulation.  
Run "compare.py" for creating a report for software simulation and functional simulation.

2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	Threshold
8	
9	:Function
10	Convert gray-scales image to binary images.  
11	Give the first output after 1 cycles while the input enable.
12	
13	:Module
14	Main module
15	
16	:Version
17	1.0
18	
19	:Modified

25	modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public

27	version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

29	This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
30	but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
31	MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
32	Lesser General Public License for more details.
33	
34	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
35	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
36	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
37	
38	Homepage for this project:
39		http://fil.dtysky.moe
40	
41	Sources for this project:
42		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
43	
44	My e-mail:
45		dtysky@outlook.com
46	
47	My blog:
48		http://dtysky.moe
49	
50	*/
51	
52	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
53	
54	
55	module Threshold(
56		clk,
57		rst_n,
58		th_mode,
59		th1,
60		th2,
61		in_enable,
62		in_data,
63		out_ready,
64		out_data
65	    );
66	
67		/*
68		::description
69		This module's working mode.
70		::range
71		0 for Pipeline, 1 for Req-ack
72		*/
73		parameter work_mode = 0;
74		/*
75		::description
76		Color's bit width.
77		::range
78		1 - 12
79		*/
80		parameter color_width = 8;
81	
82		/*
83		::description
84		Clock.
85		*/
86		input clk;
87		/*
88		::description
89		Reset, active low.
90		*/
91		input rst_n;
92		/*
93		::description
94		The method for processing.
95		::range
96		0 for Base, 1 for Contour
97		*/
98		input th_mode;
99		/*
100		::description
101		First thorshold, used for all methods.
102		*/
103		input [color_width - 1 : 0] th1;
104		/*
105		::description
106		Second thorshold, only used for "Contour" method.
107		*/
108		input [color_width - 1 : 0] th2;
109		/*
110		::description
111		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be changes.
112		*/
113		input in_enable;
114		/*
115		::description
116		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
117		*/
118		input [color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
119		/*
120		::description
121		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
122		*/
123		output out_ready;
124		/*
125		::description
126		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
127		*/
128		output out_data;
129	
130		reg reg_out_ready;
131		reg reg_out_data;
132	
133		generate
134	
135			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
136				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) begin
137					reg_out_ready <= 0;
138				end else begin
139					reg_out_ready <= 1;
140				end
141			end
142	
143			if(work_mode == 0) begin 
144	
145				always @(posedge clk) begin
146					case (th_mode)
147						0 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 ? 1 : 0;
148						1 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 && in_data <= th2 ? 1 : 0;
149						default : /* default */;
150					endcase
151				end
152	
153			end else begin
154	
155				always @(posedge in_enable) begin
156					case (th_mode)
157						0 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 ? 1 : 0;
158						1 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 && in_data <= th2 ? 1 : 0;
159						default : /* default */;
160					endcase
161				end
162	
163			end
164	
165			assign out_ready = reg_out_ready;
166			assign out_data = out_ready == 0 ? 0 : reg_out_data;
167	
168		endgenerate
169	
170	endmodule

//Com2DocHDL
/*
:Project
FPGA-Imaging-Library

:Design
Threshold

:Function
Convert gray-scales image to binary images.  
Give the first output after 1 cycles while the input enable.

:Module
Main module

:Version
1.0

:Modified
2015-05-15

Copyright (C) 2015  Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>

This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
Lesser General Public License for more details.

You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA

Homepage for this project:
	http://fil.dtysky.moe

Sources for this project:
	https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library

My e-mail:
	dtysky@outlook.com

My blog:
	http://dtysky.moe

*/

`timescale 1ns / 1ps


module Threshold(
	clk,
	rst_n,
	th_mode,
	th1,
	th2,
	in_enable,
	in_data,
	out_ready,
	out_data
    );

	/*
	::description
	This module's working mode.
	::range
	0 for Pipeline, 1 for Req-ack
	*/
	parameter work_mode = 0;
	/*
	::description
	Color's bit width.
	::range
	1 - 12
	*/
	parameter color_width = 8;

	/*
	::description
	Clock.
	*/
	input clk;
	/*
	::description
	Reset, active low.
	*/
	input rst_n;
	/*
	::description
	The method for processing.
	::range
	0 for Base, 1 for Contour
	*/
	input th_mode;
	/*
	::description
	First thorshold, used for all methods.
	*/
	input [color_width - 1 : 0] th1;
	/*
	::description
	Second thorshold, only used for "Contour" method.
	*/
	input [color_width - 1 : 0] th2;
	/*
	::description
	Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be changes.
	*/
	input in_enable;
	/*
	::description
	Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
	*/
	input [color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
	/*
	::description
	Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
	*/
	output out_ready;
	/*
	::description
	Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
	*/
	output out_data;

	reg reg_out_ready;
	reg reg_out_data;

	generate

		always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
			if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) begin
				reg_out_ready <= 0;
			end else begin
				reg_out_ready <= 1;
			end
		end

		if(work_mode == 0) begin 

			always @(posedge clk) begin
				case (th_mode)
					0 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 ? 1 : 0;
					1 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 && in_data <= th2 ? 1 : 0;
					default : /* default */;
				endcase
			end

		end else begin

			always @(posedge in_enable) begin
				case (th_mode)
					0 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 ? 1 : 0;
					1 : reg_out_data <= in_data > th1 && in_data <= th2 ? 1 : 0;
					default : /* default */;
				endcase
			end

		end

		assign out_ready = reg_out_ready;
		assign out_data = out_ready == 0 ? 0 : reg_out_data;

	endgenerate

endmodule

content.thinking:
Now I understand the standardized interface clearly. The key pattern:

**Standardized IP interface:**
- Parameters: `work_mode` (0=Pipeline, 1=Req-ack), `color_channels` (1=gray, 3=rgb), `color_width` (1-12)
- Inputs: `clk`, `rst_n` (active low), `in_enable`, `in_data`
- Outputs: `out_ready`, `out_data`
- The `::description` comments are parsed by `Com2DocHDL` tool to generate documentation/XGUI.

**Working modes:**
- Pipeline mode (work_mode=0): output registered on posedge clk, gives output after N cycles
- Req-ack mode (work_mode=1): output registered on posedge in_enable, only changes when in_enable is high

**The reset behavior:** `always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable)` — falling edge of in_enable acts as reset too.

Now let me read the core generators: FrameController, RowsGenerator, WindowGenerator. These are the "data feeding" infrastructure. Let me also read a software sim file and a create.py / compare.py to understand the simulation methodology. And a LocalFilter (MeanFilter) and a Geometry (Crop, simplest).

Let me batch-read these.

1	//Com2DocHDL
2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	WindowGenerator
8	
9	:Function
10	Generate window. 
11	The lowest "color_width" bits of "out_data" is the top left corner pixel of the window! 
12	In pipeline mode, it will give the first output after window_width / 2 + 1 cycles while the input enable. 
13	In req-ack mode, before the first window can be output, it will give a input ack for every req, then you can give the next input data. 
14	
15	:Module
16	Main module
17	
18	:Version
19	1.0
20	
21	:Modified
22	2015-05-19
23	
24	Copyright (C) 2015 Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>
25	
26	This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
27	modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
28	License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
29	version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
30	
31	This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
32	but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
33	MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
34	Lesser General Public License for more details.
35	
36	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
37	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
38	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
39	
40	Homepage for this project:
41		http://fil.dtysky.moe
42	
43	Sources for this project:
44		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
45	
46	My e-mail:
47		dtysky@outlook.com
48	
49	My blog:
50		http://dtysky.moe
51	
52	*/
53	
54	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
55	
56	module WindowGenerator(
57		clk,
58		rst_n,
59		in_enable,
60		in_data,
61		out_ready,
62		out_data,
63		input_ack
64		);
65	
66		/*
67		::description
68		This module's working mode.
69		::range
70		0 for Pipline, 1 for Req-ack
71		*/
72		parameter[0 : 0] work_mode = 0;
73		/*
74		::description
75		The width(and height) of window.
76		::range
77		2 - 15
78		*/
79		parameter[3 : 0] window_width = 3;
80		/*
81		::description
82		Color's bit wide.
83		::range
84		1 - 12
85		*/
86		parameter[3: 0] color_width = 8;
87	
88		parameter[2 : 0] window_width_half = window_width >> 1;
89		/*
90		::description
91		Clock.
92		*/
93		input clk;
94		/*
95		::description
96		Reset, active low.
97		*/
98		input rst_n;
99		/*
100		::description
101		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be really changes.
102		*/
103		input in_enable;
104		/*
105		::description
106		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
107		*/
108		input [color_width * window_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
109		/*
110		::description
111		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
112		*/
113		output out_ready;
114		/*
115		::description
116		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
117		*/
118		output[color_width * window_width * window_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
119		/*
120		::description
121		Input ack, only used for req-ack mode, this port will give a ack while the input_data received. 
122		*/
123		output input_ack;
124	
125		reg[color_width * window_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data[0 : window_width - 1];
126		reg[3 : 0] con_init;
127	
128	
129		genvar y;
130		genvar x;
131		generate
132			if(work_mode == 0) begin
133				assign input_ack = 0;
134				reg reg_out_ready;
135				assign out_ready = reg_out_ready;
136				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
137					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) begin
138						con_init <= 0;
139						reg_out_ready <= 0;
140					end else if(con_init == window_width_half) begin
141						con_init <= con_init;
142						reg_out_ready <= 1;
143					end else begin
144						con_init <= con_init + 1;
145						reg_out_ready <= 0;
146					end
147				end
148				for (y = 0; y < window_width; y = y + 1) begin
149					for (x = 0; x < window_width; x = x + 1) begin
150						if (x == 0) begin 
151							always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
152								if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
153									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= 0;
154								else
155									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= in_data[(y + 1) * color_width - 1 : y * color_width];
156							end
157						end else begin
158							always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
159								if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
160									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= 0;
161								else
162									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= reg_out_data[y][x * color_width - 1 : (x - 1)* color_width];
163							end
164						end
165					end
166					assign out_data[(y + 1) * color_width * window_width - 1 : y * color_width * window_width] = 
167						out_ready ? reg_out_data[y] : 0;
168				end
169	
170			end else begin
171	
172				reg in_enable_last;
173				always @(posedge clk)
174					in_enable_last <= in_enable;
175				reg reg_input_ack;
176				assign input_ack = reg_input_ack;
177				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
178					if(~rst_n)
179						con_init <= 0;
180					else if(con_init == window_width_half + 1)
181						con_init <= con_init;
182					else if(~in_enable_last & in_enable)
183						con_init <= con_init + 1;
184					else
185						con_init <= con_init;
186				end
187				assign out_ready = con_init == window_width_half + 1 ? 1 : 0;
188				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
189					if(~rst_n)
190						reg_input_ack <= 0;
191					else if(~in_enable_last & in_enable)
192						reg_input_ack <= 1;
193					else if(in_enable_last & ~in_enable)
194						reg_input_ack <= 0;
195				end
196	
197				for (y = 0; y < window_width; y = y + 1) begin
198					for (x = 0; x < window_width; x = x + 1) begin
199						if (x == 0) begin 
200							always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
201								if(~rst_n)
202									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= 0;
203								else if(~in_enable_last & in_enable)
204									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= in_data[(y + 1) * color_width - 1 : y * color_width];
205								else
206									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width];
207							end
208						end else begin
209							always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
210								if(~rst_n)
211									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= 0;
212								else if(~in_enable_last & in_enable)
213									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= reg_out_data[y][x * color_width - 1 : (x - 1)* color_width];
214								else 
215									reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width] <= reg_out_data[y][(x + 1) * color_width - 1 : x * color_width];
216							end
217						end
218					end
219					assign out_data[(y + 1) * color_width * window_width - 1 : y * color_width * window_width] = 
220						out_ready ? reg_out_data[y] : 0;
221				end
222	
223			end
224			
225		endgenerate
226	
227	endmodule

1	//Com2DocHDL
2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	RowsGenerator
8	
9	:Function
10	Generate rows cache, **this module just support Pipeline mode now !!!** 
11	The lowest color_width-bits of out_data are the first row! 
12	You can configure all fifos by yourself, but fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
13	And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 ! 
14	Give the first output after rows_width * (rows_depth + 1) cycles while the input enable.
15	
16	:Module
17	Main module
18	
19	:Version
20	1.0
21	
22	:Modified
23	2015-05-18
24	
25	Copyright (C) 2015 Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>
26	
27	This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
28	modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
29	License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
30	version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
31	
32	This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
33	but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
34	MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
35	Lesser General Public License for more details.
36	
37	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
38	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
39	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
40	
41	Homepage for this project:
42		http://fil.dtysky.moe
43	
44	Sources for this project:
45		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
46	
47	My e-mail:
48		dtysky@outlook.com
49	
50	My blog:
51		http://dtysky.moe
52	
53	*/
54	
55	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
56	
57	module RowsGenerator(
58		clk,
59		rst_n,
60		in_enable,
61		in_data,
62		out_ready,
63		out_data
64		);
65	
66		/*
67		::description
68		The width of rows.
69		::range
70		2 - 15
71		*/
72		parameter[3 : 0] rows_width = 3;
73		/*
74		::description
75		The width of image.
76		::range
77		1 - 4096
78		*/
79		parameter im_width = 320;
80		/*
81		::description
82		Color's bit wide.
83		::range
84		1 - 12
85		*/
86		parameter[3: 0] color_width = 8;
87		/*
88		::description
89		The bits of width of image.
90		::range
91		Depend on width of image
92		*/
93		parameter[4 : 0] im_width_bits = 9;
94	
95		/*
96		::description
97		Clock.
98		*/
99		input clk;
100		/*
101		::description
102		Reset, active low.
103		*/
104		input rst_n;
105		/*
106		::description
107		Input data enable, it works as fifo0's wr_en.
108		*/
109		input in_enable;
110		/*
111		::description
112		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
113		*/
114		input [color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
115		/*
116		::description
117		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
118		*/
119		output out_ready;
120		/*
121		::description
122		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready. 
123		The lowest color_width-bits of this are the first row! 
124		*/
125		output[rows_width * color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
126	
127		reg reg_row_wr_en[0 : rows_width];
128		wire row_wr_en[0 : rows_width];
129		wire row_rd_en[0 : rows_width - 1];
130		wire[color_width - 1 : 0] row_din[0 : rows_width - 1];
131		wire[color_width - 1 : 0] row_dout[0 : rows_width - 1];
132		wire[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] row_num[0 : rows_width - 1];
133	
134		wire rst = ~rst_n;
135	
136		genvar i, j;
137		generate
138			assign out_ready = row_wr_en[rows_width];
139	
140			for (i = 0; i < rows_width; i = i + 1) begin : fifos
141				
142				assign row_rd_en[i] = row_num[i] == im_width - 1 ? 1 : 0;
143	
144				if (i == 0) begin
145					assign row_wr_en[i] = in_enable;
146					assign row_din[i] = in_data;
147				end else begin 
148					assign row_din[i] = row_dout[i - 1];
149				end
150	
151				//One clock delay from fifo read enable to data out
152				always @(posedge clk)
153					reg_row_wr_en[i + 1] <= row_rd_en[i];
154				assign row_wr_en[i + 1] = reg_row_wr_en[i + 1];
155	
156				case (color_width)
157					1 : 
158						/*
159						::description
160						Fifo which has 1 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 1. 
161						You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
162						And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
163						*/
164						Fifo1xWidthRows Fifo(
165						.clk(clk),
166						.rst(rst),
167						.din(row_din[i]),
168						.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
169						.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
170						.dout(row_dout[i]),
171						.data_count(row_num[i])
172						);
173					2, 3, 4 : 
174						/*
175						::description
176						Fifo which has 4 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 2, 3 and 4. 
177						You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
178						And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
179						*/
180						Fifo4xWidthRows Fifo(
181						.clk(clk),
182						.rst(rst),
183						.din(row_din[i]),
184						.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
185						.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
186						.dout(row_dout[i]),
187						.data_count(row_num[i])
188						);
189					5, 6, 7, 8 : 
190						/*
191						::description
192						Fifo which has 8 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 5, 6, 7 and 8. 
193						You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
194						And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
195						*/
196						Fifo8xWidthRows Fifo(
197						.clk(clk),
198						.rst(rst),
199						.din(row_din[i]),
200						.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
201						.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
202						.dout(row_dout[i]),
203						.data_count(row_num[i])
204						);
205					9, 10, 11, 12 : 
206						/*
207						::description
208						Fifo which has 12 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 9, 10, 11 and 12. 
209						You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
210						And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
211						*/
212						Fifo12xWidthRows Fifo(
213						.clk(clk),
214						.rst(rst),
215						.din(row_din[i]),
216						.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
217						.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
218						.dout(row_dout[i]),
219						.data_count(row_num[i])
220						);
221					default : /* default */;
222				endcase
223	
224				assign out_data[(i + 1) * color_width - 1 : i * color_width] = out_ready ? row_dout[rows_width - 1 - i] : 0;
225			
226			end
227	
228		endgenerate
229	
230	endmodule

RowsGenerator

:Function

And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 ! 
Give the first output after rows_width * (rows_depth + 1) cycles while the input enable.

:Module

My e-mail:
	dtysky@outlook.com

My blog:
	http://dtysky.moe

*/

`timescale 1ns / 1ps

module RowsGenerator(
	clk,
	rst_n,
	in_enable,
	in_data,
	out_ready,
	out_data
	);

	/*
	::description
	The width of rows.
	::range
	2 - 15
	*/
	parameter[3 : 0] rows_width = 3;
	/*
	::description
	The width of image.
	::range
	1 - 4096
	*/
	parameter im_width = 320;
	/*
	::description
	Color's bit wide.
	::range
	1 - 12
	*/
	parameter[3: 0] color_width = 8;
	/*
	::description
	The bits of width of image.
	::range
	Depend on width of image
	*/
	parameter[4 : 0] im_width_bits = 9;

	/*
	::description
	Clock.
	*/
	input clk;
	/*
	::description
	Reset, active low.
	*/
	input rst_n;
	/*
	::description
	Input data enable, it works as fifo0's wr_en.
	*/
	input in_enable;
	/*
	::description
	Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
	*/
	input [color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
	/*
	::description
	Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
	*/
	output out_ready;
	/*
	::description
	Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready. 
	The lowest color_width-bits of this are the first row! 
	*/
	output[rows_width * color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;

	reg reg_row_wr_en[0 : rows_width];
	wire row_wr_en[0 : rows_width];
	wire row_rd_en[0 : rows_width - 1];
	wire[color_width - 1 : 0] row_din[0 : rows_width - 1];
	wire[color_width - 1 : 0] row_dout[0 : rows_width - 1];
	wire[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] row_num[0 : rows_width - 1];

	wire rst = ~rst_n;

	genvar i, j;
	generate
		assign out_ready = row_wr_en[rows_width];

		for (i = 0; i < rows_width; i = i + 1) begin : fifos
			
			assign row_rd_en[i] = row_num[i] == im_width - 1 ? 1 : 0;

			if (i == 0) begin
				assign row_wr_en[i] = in_enable;
				assign row_din[i] = in_data;
			end else begin 
				assign row_din[i] = row_dout[i - 1];
			end

			//One clock delay from fifo read enable to data out
			always @(posedge clk)
				reg_row_wr_en[i + 1] <= row_rd_en[i];
			assign row_wr_en[i + 1] = reg_row_wr_en[i + 1];

			case (color_width)
				1 : 
					/*
					::description
					Fifo which has 1 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 1. 
					You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
					And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
					*/
					Fifo1xWidthRows Fifo(
					.clk(clk),
					.rst(rst),
					.din(row_din[i]),
					.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
					.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
					.dout(row_dout[i]),
					.data_count(row_num[i])
					);
				2, 3, 4 : 
					/*
					::description
					Fifo which has 4 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 2, 3 and 4. 
					You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
					And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
					*/
					Fifo4xWidthRows Fifo(
					.clk(clk),
					.rst(rst),
					.din(row_din[i]),
					.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
					.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
					.dout(row_dout[i]),
					.data_count(row_num[i])
					);
				5, 6, 7, 8 : 
					/*
					::description
					Fifo which has 8 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 5, 6, 7 and 8. 
					You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
					And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
					*/
					Fifo8xWidthRows Fifo(
					.clk(clk),
					.rst(rst),
					.din(row_din[i]),
					.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
					.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
					.dout(row_dout[i]),
					.data_count(row_num[i])
					);
				9, 10, 11, 12 : 
					/*
					::description
					Fifo which has 12 width and N depth (0 < N < 4096), used for rows cache which color_width is 9, 10, 11 and 12. 
					You can configure the fifo by yourself, but all fifos in one project whcih have same name must have same configurations. 
					And you can just change the "Write Depth" and "Fifo Implementation", the Read Latency must be 1 !
					*/
					Fifo12xWidthRows Fifo(
					.clk(clk),
					.rst(rst),
					.din(row_din[i]),
					.wr_en(row_wr_en[i]),
					.rd_en(row_rd_en[i]),
					.dout(row_dout[i]),
					.data_count(row_num[i])
					);
				default : /* default */;
			endcase

			assign out_data[(i + 1) * color_width - 1 : i * color_width] = out_ready ? row_dout[rows_width - 1 - i] : 0;
		
		end

	endgenerate

endmodule

file.content:
# Wiki and Contact
[Homepage for this project](http://ifl.dtysky.moe)  
[Sources for this project](https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library)  
[My e-mail](Mailto:dtysky@outlook.com)  
[My blog](http://dtysky.moe)  

# Simulations
**Simulations for this module just support 512xN, Gray-scale and binary images and conf['width'] only can be 3 and 5 !**  

## Steps for simulations
**Warning: procedures for simulation depend on python 2.7 and PIL(Python Imaging Library), please get them by your self.**  

### Preparing
Open "ImageForTest".  
Put images which you want to use for simulations.  
Edit the "conf.json" for setting the configurations for simulations.  

### Software simulation
Open "SoftwareSim".  
Run "sim.py".  
Open "SimResCheck", check your results.  

### Creat preparing data for functional simulation
Open "HDLSimDataGen".  
Run "creat.py".  

### Functional simulation
Functional simulation just support for modelsim 10.1 up.   
Above all, you must compile all xilinx vivado library to modelsim.  

Open "FunSimForHDL".  
Open "ContrastTranslate.mpf" with modelsim.  

#### Do these:

Run this commond at first time:

    vlib work  

Then, compile all your source file:  

    modelsim gui -> Compile -> Compile all  

Then, run this:

    do Run.do

If you just want to the finally result, and don't want to watch the waveform, run this:  

    do RunOver.do

## Comparing
Open "SimResCheck".  
Run "covert.py", then you can get the results form functional simulation.  
Run "compare.py" for creating a report for software simulation and functional simulation.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the core data-feeding infrastructure:
- **RowsGenerator**: Uses FIFOs (Fifo1xWidthRows, Fifo4xWidthRows, Fifo8xWidthRows, Fifo12xWidthRows based on color_width) to cache image rows. Outputs `rows_width` consecutive rows in parallel (lowest bits = first row). Only Pipeline mode. Latency: `rows_width * (rows_depth + 1)` cycles.
- **WindowGenerator**: Takes `rows_width` rows from RowsGenerator and produces a `window_width × window_width` window via shift registers. Supports both Pipeline and Req-ack modes. In req-ack mode uses `input_ack` for handshaking.

This is the classic "line buffer + window" pattern for FPGA image processing.

Now I need to understand:
1. FrameController / FrameController2 (frame-level addressing for geometry transforms that need random access)
2. A LocalFilter (MeanFilter - uses WindowGenerator)
3. A Geometry transform that needs random access (Rotate/Scale use FrameController2 + Multipliers)
4. The simulation methodology (sim.py, create.py, compare.py)
5. The Connector modules and how data flows
6. The on-board test setup (TestOnBoard + BoardInit_AXI)
7. Com2DocHDL documentation generation / xgui tcl

Let me read more files. I'll batch the most important ones. Given budget, let me be strategic. Let me delegate parallel reads to agents to gather details efficiently while I focus on synthesis.

Actually, I've already understood the core patterns well. Let me read a few more key files directly to confirm specifics, then I can design the manifest. Let me read:
- MeanFilter.v (LocalFilter example with window)
- Crop.v and a software sim.py + create.py + compare.py to understand methodology
- FrameController README + FrameController2.v briefly
- Graying.v (uses multipliers, RGB→Gray)
- CountGenerator.v
- A connector like Mux2.v and DataWidthConvert.v
- TestOnBoard/Point/ForBuild/Main.c and build.tcl
- component.xml and xgui tcl for one IP to understand packaging

Let me dispatch this in parallel using agents to conserve my own context, but actually I think direct reads are more reliable for getting exact source_files paths. Let me do targeted reads of the most representative files. I'll batch them.

1	//Com2DocHDL
2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	MeanFilter
8	
9	:Function
10	Local filter - Mean filter, it always used for smoothing images. 
11	It will give the first output after sum_stage + 1 cycles while the input enable. 
12	
13	:Module
14	Main module
15	
16	:Version
17	1.0
18	
19	:Modified
20	2015-05-20
21	
22	Copyright (C) 2015 Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>
23	
24	This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
25	modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
26	License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
27	version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
28	
29	This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
30	but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
31	MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
32	Lesser General Public License for more details.
33	
34	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
35	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
36	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
37	
38	Homepage for this project:
39		http://fil.dtysky.moe
40	
41	Sources for this project:
42		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
43	
44	My e-mail:
45		dtysky@outlook.com
46	
47	My blog:
48		http://dtysky.moe
49	
50	*/
51	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
52	`define full_win_width window_width * window_width
53	
54	module MeanFilter(
55		clk, 
56		rst_n, 
57		in_enable, 
58		in_data, 
59		out_ready, 
60		out_data);
61	
62		/*
63		::description
64		This module's working mode.
65		::range
66		0 for Pipeline, 1 for Req-ack
67		*/
68		parameter[0 : 0] work_mode = 0;
69		/*
70		::description
71		The width(and height) of window.
72		::range
73		2 - 15
74		*/
75		parameter[3 : 0] window_width = 3;
76		/*
77		::description
78		Color's bit wide.
79		::range
80		1 - 12
81		*/
82		parameter[3: 0] color_width = 8;
83		/*
84		::description
85		Stage of sum.
86		::range
87		Depend on width of window, log2(window_width^2)
88		*/
89		parameter sum_stage = 3;
90	
91		/*
92		::description
93		Clock.
94		*/
95		input clk;
96		/*
97		::description
98		Reset, active low.
99		*/
100		input rst_n;
101		/*
102		::description
103		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be really changes.
104		*/
105		input in_enable;
106		/*
107		::description
108		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
109		*/
110		input [color_width * window_width * window_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
111		/*
112		::description
113		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
114		*/
115		output out_ready;
116		/*
117		::description
118		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
119		*/
120		output[color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
121	
122		reg[color_width - 1 : 0] reg_in_data[0 : `full_win_width - 1];
123		reg[color_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data;
124		wire[color_width + `full_win_width - 1 : 0] sum_all;
125		reg[3 : 0] con_enable;
126	
127		genvar i, j;
128		generate
129	
130			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
131				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) 
132					con_enable <= 0;
133				else if(con_enable == sum_stage + 1)
134					con_enable <= con_enable;
135				else
136					con_enable <= con_enable + 1;
137			end
138			assign out_ready = con_enable == sum_stage + 1 ? 1 : 0;
139	
140	
141			for (i = 0; i < `full_win_width; i = i + 1) begin
142				if(work_mode == 0) begin
143					always @(*)
144						reg_in_data[i] = in_data[(i + 1) * color_width - 1 : i * color_width];
145				end else begin
146					always @(posedge in_enable)
147						reg_in_data[i] = in_data[(i + 1) * color_width - 1 : i * color_width];
148				end
149	
150			end
151	
152			for (i = 0; i < sum_stage; i = i + 1) begin : pipe
153				reg[color_width + `full_win_width - 1 : 0] sum[0 : (`full_win_width >> i + 1) - 1];
154				for (j = 0; j < `full_win_width >> i + 1; j = j + 1) begin
155					if(i == 0) begin
156	
157						if(j == 0 && ((`full_win_width >> i) % 2 != 0)) begin
158							always @(posedge clk)
159								sum[j] <= 
160									reg_in_data[`full_win_width - 1] +
161									reg_in_data[2 * j] +
162									reg_in_data[2 * j + 1];
163						end else begin
164							always @(posedge clk)
165								sum[j] <= 
166									reg_in_data[2 * j] +
167									reg_in_data[2 * j + 1];
168						end
169	
170					end else begin 
171	
172						if(j == 0 && ((`full_win_width >> i) % 2 != 0)) begin
173							always @(posedge clk)
174								sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].sum[(`full_win_width >> i) - 1] + pipe[i - 1].sum[2 * j] + pipe[i - 1].sum[2 * j + 1];
175						end else begin
176							always @(posedge clk)
177								sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].sum[2 * j] + pipe[i - 1].sum[2 * j + 1];
178						end
179					end
180	
181				end
182			end
183	
184			assign sum_all = pipe[sum_stage - 1].sum[0];
185			case (window_width)
186			 	2 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= sum_all >> 2;
187			 	3 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 4) + (sum_all >> 5) + (sum_all >> 6);
188			 	4 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= sum_all >> 4;
189			 	5 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 5) + (sum_all >> 7) + (sum_all >> 10);
190			 	6 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 6) + (sum_all >> 7) + (sum_all >> 8);
191			 	7 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 6) + (sum_all >> 8) + (sum_all >> 10);
192			 	8 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= sum_all >> 6;
193			 	9 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 7) + (sum_all >> 8) + (sum_all >> 11);
194			 	10 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 7) + (sum_all >> 9) + (sum_all >> 13);
195			 	11 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 7) + (sum_all >> 12) + (sum_all >> 13);
196			 	12 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 8) + (sum_all >> 9) + (sum_all >> 10);
197			 	13 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 8) + (sum_all >> 9) + (sum_all >> 14);
198			 	14 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 8) + (sum_all >> 10) + (sum_all >> 12);
199			 	15 : always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= (sum_all >> 8) + (sum_all >> 11);
200			 	default : /* default */;
201			endcase
202	
203			assign out_data = out_ready ? reg_out_data : 0;
204	
205		endgenerate
206	
207	endmodule
208	`undef full_win_width

11	Give the first output after 1 cycle while the input enable.
12	
13	:Module
14	Main module
15	
16	:Version
17	1.0

32	Lesser General Public License for more details.
33	
34	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
35	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
36	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
37	
38	Homepage for this project:
39		http://fil.dtysky.moe
40	
41	Sources for this project:
42		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
43	
44	My e-mail:
45		dtysky@outlook.com
46	
47	My blog:
48		http://dtysky.moe
49	*/
50	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
51	
52	module Crop(
53		clk,
54		rst_n,
55		top,
56		bottom,
57		left,
58		right,
59		in_enable,
60		in_data,
61		in_count_x,
62		in_count_y,
63		out_ready,
64		out_data,
65		out_count_x,
66		out_count_y);
67		/*
68		::description
69		This module's working mode.
70		::range
71		0 for Pipline, 1 for Req-ack
72		*/
73		parameter work_mode = 0;
74		/*
75		::description
76		Data bit width. 
77		*/
78		parameter data_width = 8;
79		/*
80		::description
81		Width of image.
82		::range
83		1 - 4096
84		*/
85		parameter im_width = 320;
86		/*
87		::description
88		Height of image.
89		::range
90		1 - 4096
91		*/
92		parameter im_height = 240;
93		/*
94		::description
95		The bits of width of image.
96		::range
97		Depend on width of image
98		*/
99		parameter im_width_bits = 9;
100	
101		/*
102		::description
103		Clock.
104		*/
105		input clk;
106		/*
107		::description
108		Reset, active low.
109		*/
110		input rst_n;
111		/*
112		::description
113		Top of the rang you want to crop. 
114		::range
115		Depend on height of image, 0 - im_height-1. 
116		*/
117		input[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] top;
118		/*
119		::description
120		Bottom of the rang you want to crop. 
121		::range
122		Depend on height of image, 0 - im_height-1. 
123		*/
124		input[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] bottom;
125		/*
126		::description
127		Left of the rang you want to crop. 
128		::range
129		Depend on height of image, 0 - im_width-1. 
130		*/
131		input[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] left;
132		/*
133		::description
134		Right of the rang you want to crop. 
135		::range
136		Depend on height of image, 0 - im_width-1. 
137		*/
138		input[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] right;
139		/*
140		::description
141		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be really changes.
142		*/
143		input in_enable;
144		/*
145		::description
146		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
147		*/
148		input [data_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
149		/*
150		::description
151		Input pixel count for width. 
152		*/
153		input[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] in_count_x;
154		/*
155		::description
156		Input pixel count for height. 
157		*/
158		input[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] in_count_y;
159		/*
160		::description
161		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
162		*/
163		output out_ready;
164		/*
165		::description
166		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
167		*/
168		output[data_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
169		/*
170		::description
171		Output pixel count for height. 
172		*/
173		output[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] out_count_x;
174		/*
175		::description
176		Output pixel count for height. 
177		*/
178		output[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] out_count_y;
179	
180		generate
181	
182			reg[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] reg_out_count_x;
183			reg[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] reg_out_count_y;
184			reg[data_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data;
185			reg reg_out_ready;
186			reg in_range_t, in_range_b, in_range_l, in_range_r;
187	
188			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
189				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
190					reg_out_ready <= 0;
191				else
192					reg_out_ready <= 1;
193			end
194			assign out_ready = reg_out_ready;
195	
196			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
197				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) begin
198					in_range_t <= 0;
199					in_range_b <= 0;
200					in_range_l <= 0;
201					in_range_r <= 0;
202				end else begin
203					in_range_t <= in_count_y >= top ? 1 : 0;
204					in_range_b <= in_count_y <= bottom ? 1 : 0;
205					in_range_l <= in_count_x >= left ? 1 : 0;
206					in_range_r <= in_count_x <= right ? 1 : 0;
207				end
208			end
209	
210			if(work_mode == 0) begin
211				always @(posedge clk) begin
212					reg_out_count_x <= in_count_x;
213					reg_out_count_y <= in_count_y;
214					reg_out_data <= in_data;
215				end
216			end else begin 
217				always @(posedge in_enable) begin
218					reg_out_count_x <= in_count_x;
219					reg_out_count_y <= in_count_y;
220					reg_out_data <= in_data;
221				end
222			end
223			assign out_count_x = reg_out_count_x;
224			assign out_count_y = reg_out_count_y;
225			assign out_data = out_ready & in_range_t & in_range_b & in_range_l & in_range_r ? reg_out_data : 0;
226	
227		endgenerate
228		
229	endmodule

"""

__author__ = 'Tianyu Dai (dtysky)'

from PIL import Image
import os, json
from ctypes import *
user32 = windll.LoadLibrary('user32.dll')
MessageBox = lambda x:user32.MessageBoxA(0, x, 'Error', 0) 

FileFormat = ['.jpg', '.bmp']
Conf = json.load(open('../ImageForTest/conf.json', 'r'))['conf']
Debug = False

def show_error(e):
	MessageBox(e)
	exit(0)

def name_format(root, name, ex, conf):
	return '%s-soft%s' % (name, '.bmp')

def transform(im, conf):
	mode = im.mode
	if mode not in ['RGB', 'L', '1']:
		show_error('This module just supports RGB, Gray-scale and binary images, check your images !')
	im_res = im.point(lambda p : 255 - p)
	return im_res

def debug(im, conf):
	mode = im.mode
	if mode not in ['RGB', 'L', '1']:
		show_error('This module just supports RGB, Gray-scale and binary images, check your images !')
	data_src = im.getdata()
	data_res = ''
	for p in data_src:
		if mode == 'RGB':
			data_res += '%d %d %d\n' % (255 - color[0], 255 - color[1], 255 - color[2])
		else:
			data_res += '%d\n' % (255 - color)
	return data_res


FileAll = []
for root, dirs, files in os.walk('../ImageForTest'):
    for f in files:
    	name, ex = os.path.splitext(f)
        if ex in FileFormat:
        	FileAll.append((root+'/', name, ex))
for root, name, ex in FileAll:
	im_src = Image.open(root + name + ex)
	for c in Conf:
		if Debug:
			open('../SimResCheck/%s.dat' \
				% name_format(root, name, ex, c), 'w').write(debug(im_src, c))
			continue
		transform(im_src, c).save('../SimResCheck/%s' % name_format(root, name, ex, c))


FileAll = []
for root, dirs, files in os.walk('../ImageForTest'):
    for f in files:
    	name, ex = os.path.splitext(f)
        if ex in FileFormat:
        	FileAll.append((root+'/', name, ex))
for root, name, ex in FileAll:
	im_src = Image.open(root + name + ex)
	for c in Conf:
		if Debug:
			open('../SimResCheck/%s.dat' \
				% name_format(root, name, ex, c), 'w').write(debug(im_src, c))
			continue
		transform(im_src, c).save('../SimResCheck/%s' % name_format(root, name, ex, c))

file.content:
__author__ = 'Tianyu Dai (dtysky)'

from PIL import Image
import os, json
from ctypes import *
user32 = windll.LoadLibrary('user32.dll')
MessageBox = lambda x:user32.MessageBoxA(0, x, 'Error', 0) 

FileFormat = ['.jpg', '.bmp']
Conf = json.load(open('../ImageForTest/conf.json', 'r'))['conf']

def show_error(e):
	MessageBox(e)
	exit(0)

def name_format(root, name, ex, conf):
	return '%s' % name

def conf_format(im, conf):
	return '%s\n' % im.mode

def color_format(mode, color):
	if mode == '1':
		return '0' if color == 0 else '1'
	if mode != 'RGB':
		color = [color]
	res = ''
	for c in color:
		tmp = bin(c)[2:]
		for i in xrange(10 - len(bin(c))):
			tmp = '0' + tmp
		res += tmp
	return res

def create_dat(im, conf):
	mode = im.mode
	if mode not in ['RGB', 'L', '1']:
		show_error('This module just supports RGB, Gray-scale and binary images, check your images !')
	data_src = im.getdata()
	xsize, ysize = im.size
	data_res = ''
	for color in data_src:
		data_res += color_format(mode, color) + '\n'
	return data_res[:-1]

FileAll = []
for root,dirs,files in os.walk('../ImageForTest'):
    for f in files:
    	name, ex=os.path.splitext(f)
        if ex in FileFormat:
        	FileAll.append((root+'/', name, ex))
dat_index = ''
for root, name, ex in FileAll:
	im_src = Image.open(root + name + ex)
	xsize, ysize = im_src.size
	for c in Conf:
		dat_res = open('../FunSimForHDL/%s.dat' \
			% name_format(root, name, ex, c), 'w')
		dat_res.write('%d\n%d\n' % (xsize, ysize))
		dat_res.write('%s' % conf_format(im_src, c))
		dat_res.write(create_dat(im_src, c))
		dat_index += '%s' % name_format(root, name, ex, c)
		dat_index += '\n'
		dat_res.close()
dat_index = dat_index[:-1]
dat_index_f = open('../FunSimForHDL/imgindex.dat','w')
dat_index_f.write(dat_index)
dat_index_f.close()

content.thinking:
Now I have a very clear understanding of the simulation methodology:

**Simulation methodology (per IP):**
1. `ImageForTest/conf.json` — configures test images and parameters
2. `SoftwareSim/sim.py` — Python (2.7 + PIL) reference implementation; reads images, applies `transform()`, writes `*-soft.bmp` to `SimResCheck/`
3. `HDLSimDataGen/create.py` — generates `.dat` files (binary pixel data) from images, writes to `FunSimForHDL/` for the HDL testbench to read; also writes `imgindex.dat`
4. `FunSimForHDL/` — ModelSim simulation: `Run.do` / `RunOver.do` scripts, `.mpf` project; testbench reads `.dat`, drives DUT, writes output `.dat`
5. `SimResCheck/convert.py` — converts HDL output `.dat` back to images
6. `SimResCheck/compare.py` — compares software sim result vs HDL sim result, generates `compare_report.txt`

**Categories of modules:**
- **Point operations**: 1-cycle latency, pure combinational-per-pixel. ColorReversal, Threshold, Graying (uses multipliers), ContrastTransform (uses multiplier), LightnessTransform (uses signed/True2Comp).
- **LocalFilter**: window-based neighborhood ops. MeanFilter (adder tree + shift-divide), RankFilter (sorting network + Encoder), ErosionDilationBin (morphology), MatchTemplateBin, ThresholdLocal (combines Mean+Rank). These consume the WindowGenerator output.
- **Geometry**: 
  - Simple streaming: Crop (range check on count_x/count_y), Mirror, Pan
  - Random-access (need frame buffer): Rotate (sin/cos LUT + signed fixed-point multipliers + FrameController2), Scale (unsigned fixed-point multiplier + FrameController2), Shear (signed fixed-point + FrameController2)
- **Generator**: CountGenerator, FrameController, FrameController2, RowsGenerator, WindowGenerator
- **Connector**: routing/data manipulation utilities
- **InOut**: hardware I/O (VGA, Camera, IIC, Bram)
- **BoardInit_AXI**: AXI4-Lite wrapper IP for Zynq (has C driver for PS side)
- **TestOnBoard**: Vivado projects + Main.c + Constraints.xdc for on-board demos per category

Now let me understand a few more things to round out the curriculum:
1. The testbench structure (`_TB.sv`) — how it drives the DUT and verifies
2. FrameController/FrameController2 — frame buffer addressing for random-access geometry
3. A connector (Mux2, DataWidthConvert) and how they fit
4. The on-board test Main.c and build.tcl
5. component.xml / xgui tcl (IP packaging)
6. Com2DocHDL — the doc-generation from `::description` comments

Let me read these in parallel. I'll prioritize: FrameController2.v, a testbench (ColorReversal_TB.sv), TestOnBoard/Point/ForBuild/Main.c, compare.py, and a component.xml + xgui tcl. And Graying.v (RGB→gray with multipliers). And Mux2.v + DataWidthConvert.v. And Rotate.v to see the random-access + fixed-point path.

That's a lot. Let me be selective and batch. Given budget ($2.63 left), I have room. Let me read the most curriculum-critical ones.

172		reg[3 : 0] con_enable;
173		reg[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] reg_in_count_x;
174		reg[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] reg_in_count_y;
175		reg[addr_width - 1 : 0] reg_addr;
176		wire[11 : 0] mul_a, mul_b;
177		wire[23 : 0] mul_p;
178		assign mul_a = {{(12 - im_width_bits){1'b0}}, in_count_y};
179		assign mul_b = im_width;
180	
181		genvar i;
182		generate
183			/*
184			::description
185			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x Unsigned 12bits, used for creating address for frame. 
186			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay". 
187			You can not change the ports' configurations!
188			*/
189			Multiplier12x12FR2 Mul(.CLK(clk), .A(mul_a), .B(mul_b), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_p));
190			for (i = 0; i < mul_delay; i = i + 1) begin : conut_buffer
191				reg[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] b;
192				if(i == 0) begin
193					always @(posedge clk)
194						b <= in_count_x;
195				end else begin
196					always @(posedge clk)
197						b <= conut_buffer[i - 1].b;
198				end
199			end
200			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
201				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) begin
202					reg_addr <= 0;
203				end else begin
204					reg_addr <= mul_p + conut_buffer[mul_delay - 1].b;
205				end
206			end
207			assign ram_addr = reg_addr;
208	
209			if(wr_mode == 0) begin
210	
211				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
212					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
213						con_enable <= 0;
214					else if(con_enable == mul_delay + 1)
215						con_enable <= con_enable;
216					else
217						con_enable <= con_enable + 1;
218				end
219				assign out_ready = con_enable == mul_delay + 1 ? 1 : 0;
220	
221				if(work_mode == 0) begin
222					for (i = 0; i < mul_delay + 1; i = i + 1) begin : buffer
223						reg[data_width - 1 : 0] b;
224						if(i == 0) begin
225							always @(posedge clk)
226								b <= in_data;
227						end else begin
228							always @(posedge clk)
229								b <= buffer[i - 1].b;
230						end
231					end
232					assign out_data = out_ready ? buffer[mul_delay].b : 0;
233				end else begin 
234					reg[data_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data;
235					always @(posedge in_enable)
236						reg_out_data = in_data;
237					assign out_data = out_ready ? reg_out_data : 0;
238				end
239	
240			end else begin
241	
242				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
243					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
244						con_enable <= 0;
245					else if (con_enable == mul_delay + 1 + ram_read_latency)
246						con_enable <= con_enable;
247					else
248						con_enable <= con_enable + 1;
249				end
250				assign out_data = out_ready ? in_data : 0;
251				assign out_ready = con_enable == mul_delay + 1 + ram_read_latency ? 1 : 0;
252	
253			end
254	
255		endgenerate
256	
257	endmodule

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding. The testbench pattern is clear: it instantiates 6 variants (RGB/Gray/Bin × Pipeline/ReqAck), reads `.dat` from `imgindex.dat`, drives inputs, writes `-pipeline.res` and `-reqack.res` output files.

FrameController2: computes RAM address = in_count_y * im_width + in_count_x using a multiplier (Multiplier12x12FR2), with wr_mode (write/read). Used for random-access geometry (Rotate/Scale/Shear) where you need to read arbitrary pixels from a frame buffer.

The on-board Main.c uses the BoardInit_AXI driver to set parameters (ThMode, Th1, Th2, CtScale as fixed-point, LmGain, Sels selector) and reset. This confirms the BoardInit_AXI is an AXI4-Lite wrapper that aggregates multiple image-processing IPs and exposes registers to the PS (Zynq).

Now let me read a few more to round out: 
- The BoardInit_AXI HDL (to understand the AXI aggregation) - the S00_AXI.v
- A connector (Mux2 + DataWidthConvert) 
- The component.xml + xgui tcl (IP packaging & Com2DocHDL)
- Graying.v (RGB→gray multipliers - a Point op with computation)
- Rotate.v (the most complex - random access + sin/cos LUT + fixed point)
- compare.py + convert.py
- FrameController.v (the original, line-based)
- CountGenerator.v
- VGA640x480 / Cam (InOut)
- RankFilter (sorting network)
- build.tcl + Constraints.xdc

That's a lot. Let me prioritize based on what makes the best curriculum. I'll delegate broad reading to an Explore agent for the remaining categories to conserve context, while I read the most critical ones directly. Actually, let me just read a focused batch of the most curriculum-representative files. I have good budget.

Let me read: Graying.v, Rotate.v, BoardInit_AXI_S00_AXI.v, Mux2.v, component.xml (one), xgui tcl (one), compare.py, build.tcl, VGA640x480.v, CountGenerator.v. That's 10 files. Let me batch them.

Actually, the AXI S00_AXI.v might be huge. Let me read it with a limit. Let me be strategic and batch.

content.text: 测试平台模式（6 种变体：RGB/Gray/Bin × Pipeline/ReqAck，读取 `.dat`，写入 `.res`）、`FrameController2`（RAM 寻址 `y*im_width+x`，wr_mode 读写）以及板级 `Main.c`（通过 `BoardInit_AXI` 驱动设置寄存器）都已经明确了。让我来阅读剩余具有代表性的文件：AXI 聚合、RGB→Gray、Rotate（最复杂的）、一个连接器、IP 打包以及比较工具。
