1	//Com2DocHDL
2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	Graying
8	
9	:Function
10	Covert RGB images to gray-scale images. 
11	Users can configure the multipliers by themselves. 
12	Give the first output after mul_delay + 2 cycles while the input enable.
13	
14	:Module
15	Main module
16	
17	:Version
18	1.0
19	
20	:Modified
21	2015-05-12
22	
23	Copyright (C) 2015 Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>
24	
25	This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
26	modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
27	License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
28	version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
29	
30	This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
31	but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
32	MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
33	Lesser General Public License for more details.
34	
35	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
36	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
37	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
38	
39	Homepage for this project:
40		http://fil.dtysky.moe
41	
42	Sources for this project:
43		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
44	
45	My e-mail:
46		dtysky@outlook.com
47	
48	My blog:
49		http://dtysky.moe
50	
51	*/
52	
53	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
54	
55	
56	module Graying(
57		clk,
58		rst_n,
59		in_enable,
60		in_data,
61		out_ready,
62		out_data
63	    );
64	
65		/*
66		::description
67		This module's working mode.
68		::range
69		0 for Pipeline, 1 for Req-ack
70		*/
71		parameter work_mode = 0;
72		/*
73		::description
74		Color's bit width.
75		::range
76		1 - 12
77		*/
78		parameter color_width = 8;
79		/*
80		::description
81		Delay for multiplier.
82		::range
83		Depend your multilpliers' configurations
84		*/
85		parameter mul_delay = 3;
86		/*
87		::description
88		Clock.
89		*/
90		input clk;
91		/*
92		::description
93		Reset, active low.
94		*/
95		input rst_n;
96		/*
97		::description
98		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be changes.
99		*/
100		input in_enable;
101		/*
102		::description
103		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
104		*/
105		input [3 * color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
106		/*
107		::description
108		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
109		*/
110		output out_ready;
111		/*
112		::description
113		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
114		*/
115		output[color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
116	
117		reg[color_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data;
118		reg[color_width - 1 : 0] r, g, b;
119		wire[10 : 0] mul_r;
120		wire[11 : 0] mul_g;
121		wire[8 : 0] mul_b;
122		reg[2 : 0] con_enable;
123	
124		genvar i;
125		generate
126	
127			if(work_mode == 0) begin
128				always @(*) begin
129					r = in_data[3 * color_width - 1 : 2 * color_width];
130					g = in_data[2 * color_width - 1 : 1 * color_width];
131					b = in_data[1 * color_width - 1 : 0 * color_width]; 
132				end
133			end else begin
134				always @(posedge in_enable) begin
135					r = in_data[3 * color_width - 1 : 2 * color_width];
136					g = in_data[2 * color_width - 1 : 1 * color_width];
137					b = in_data[1 * color_width - 1 : 0 * color_width];
138				end
139			end
140		
141			/*
142			::description
143			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x 0.299, used for red channel. 
144			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay". 
145			All Multiplier's pipeline stage must be same, you can not change the ports' configurations!
146			*/
147			MultiplierRedx0d299 MulRed(.CLK (clk), .A({{12 - color_width{1'b0}} ,r}), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_r));
148			/*
149			::description
150			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x 0.587, used for green channel.
151			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay".
152			All Multiplier's pipeline stage must be same, you can not change the ports' configurations!
153			*/
154			MultiplierGreenx0d587 MulGreen(.CLK (clk), .A({{12 - color_width{1'b0}} ,g}), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_g));
155			/*
156			::description
157			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x 0.114, used for blue channel.
158			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay".
159			All Multiplier's pipeline stage must be same, you can not change the ports' configurations!
160			*/
161			MultiplierBluex0d114 MulBlue(.CLK (clk), .A({{12 - color_width{1'b0}} ,b}), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_b));
162	
163			reg[11 : 0] mul_g_buffer;
164			reg[color_width - 1 : 0] sum_tmp;
165			always @(posedge clk) begin
166				mul_g_buffer <= mul_g;
167				sum_tmp <= mul_r + mul_b;
168			end
169			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
170				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) begin
171					reg_out_data <= 0;
172				end else begin
173					reg_out_data <= sum_tmp + mul_g_buffer;
174				end
175			end
176			assign out_data = out_ready ? reg_out_data : 0;
177	
178			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
179				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
180					con_enable <= 0;
181				else if(con_enable == mul_delay + 2)
182					con_enable <= con_enable;
183				else
184					con_enable <= con_enable + 1;
185			end
186			assign out_ready = con_enable == mul_delay + 2 ? 1 : 0; 
187	
188		endgenerate
189	
190	endmodule

2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	Rotate
8	
9	:Function
10	Function: Rotating an image by your given angle. 
11	Give the first output after 1 cycle while the frame input ready.
12	
13	:Module
14	Main module
15	
16	:Version
17	1.0
18	
19	:Modified
20	2015-05-28
21	
22	Copyright (C) 2015  Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>

45		dtysky@outlook.com
46	
47	My blog:
48		http://dtysky.moe
49	
50	*/
51	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
52	`define im_width_half im_width / 2
53	`define im_height_half im_height / 2
54	
55	module Rotate(
56		clk,
57		rst_n,
58		angle,
59		in_enable,
60		frame_in_ready,
61		frame_in_data,
62		frame_enable,
63		frame_out_count_x,
64		frame_out_count_y,
65		out_ready,
66		out_data);
67		/*
68		::description
69		This module's working mode.
70		::range
71		0 for Pipline, 1 for Req-ack
72		*/
73		parameter work_mode = 0;
74		/*
75		::description
76		Data bit width. 
77		*/
78		parameter data_width = 8;
79		/*
80		::description
81		Width of image.
82		::range
83		1 - 4096
84		*/
85		parameter signed[11 : 0] im_width = 320;
86		/*
87		::description
88		Height of image.
89		::range
90		1 - 4096
91		*/
92		parameter signed[11 : 0] im_height = 240;
93		/*
94		::description
95		The bits of width of image.
96		::range
97		Depend on width of image
98		*/
99		parameter im_width_bits = 9;
100		/*
101		::description
102		Delay for multiplier.
103		::range
104		Depend on your multilpliers' configurations, 1 - 14
105		*/
106		parameter mul_delay = 3;
107		/*
108		::description
109		Delay for FrameController.
110		::range
111		Depend on your FrameController 
112		*/
113		parameter ram_RL = 7;
114		/*
115		::description
116		Clock.
117		*/
118		input clk;
119		/*
120		::description
121		Reset, active low.
122		*/
123		input rst_n;
124		/*
125		::description
126		Angle. 
127		::range
128		0 - 359 
129		*/
130		input[8 : 0] angle;
131		/*
132		::description
133		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be really changes.
134		*/
135		input in_enable;
136		/*
137		::description
138		Connect to out_ready in FrameController. 
139		*/
140		input frame_in_ready;
141		/*
142		::description
143		Connect to out_data in FrameController. 
144		*/
145		input[data_width - 1 : 0] frame_in_data;
146		/*
147		::description
148		Connect to in_enable in FrameController. 
149		*/
150		output frame_enable;
151		/*
152		::description
153		Connect to in_count_u in FrameController. 
154		*/
155		output[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] frame_out_count_x;
156		/*
157		::description
158		Connect to in_count_v in FrameController. 
159		*/
160		output[im_width_bits - 1 : 0] frame_out_count_y;
161		/*
162		::description
163		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
164		*/
165		output out_ready;
166		/*
167		::description
168		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
169		*/
170		output[data_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
171	
172		wire signed[19 : 0] sina, cosa;
173		reg  signed[im_width_bits : 0] count_u, count_v;
174		reg in_range_t, in_range_b, in_range_l, in_range_r;
175		wire signed [32 : 0] mul_x_p1, mul_x_p2, mul_y_p1, mul_y_p2;
176		reg signed [12 : 0] mul_x_a1, mul_x_a2, mul_y_a1, mul_y_a2;
177		wire signed [im_width_bits : 0] mul_x_r1, mul_x_r2, mul_y_r1, mul_y_r2;
178		reg signed [im_width_bits + 1 : 0] add_x, add_y;
179		reg signed[im_width_bits + 1 : 0] reg_frame_out_count_x, reg_frame_out_count_y;
180		reg[3 : 0] con_mul_enable;
181		reg reg_out_ready;
182		reg[data_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data;
183	
184		generate
185			if(work_mode == 0) begin
186				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
187					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
188						count_u <= 0;
189					else if(count_u == im_width - 1)
190						count_u <= 0;
191					else
192						count_u <= count_u + 1;
193				end
194				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
195					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
196						count_v <= 0;
197					else if(count_u == im_width - 1 && count_v == im_height - 1)
198						count_v <= 0;
199					else if(count_u == im_width - 1)
200						count_v <= count_v + 1;
201					else
202						count_v <= count_v;
203				end
204				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
205					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
206						con_mul_enable <= 0;
207					else if(con_mul_enable == mul_delay + 6)
208						con_mul_enable <= con_mul_enable;
209					else
210						con_mul_enable <= con_mul_enable + 1;
211				end
212				assign frame_enable = con_mul_enable == mul_delay + 6 ? 1 : 0;
213			end else begin 
214				reg in_enable_last;
215				always @(posedge clk)
216					in_enable_last <= in_enable;
217				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
218					if(~rst_n)
219						count_u <= -1;
220					else if(~in_enable_last & in_enable) begin
221						if(count_u == im_width - 1)
222							count_u <= 0;
223						else
224							count_u <= count_u + 1;
225					end else
226						count_u <= count_u;
227				end
228				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
229					if(~rst_n)
230						count_v <= 0;
231					else if(~in_enable_last & in_enable) begin
232						if(count_u == im_width - 1 && count_v == im_height - 1)
233							count_v <= 0;
234						else if(count_u == im_width - 1)
235							count_v <= count_v + 1;
236						else
237							count_v <= count_v;
238					end	else
239						count_v <= count_v;
240				end
241				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
242					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
243						con_mul_enable <= 0;
244					else if(con_mul_enable == mul_delay + 7)
245						con_mul_enable <= con_mul_enable;
246					else
247						con_mul_enable <= con_mul_enable + 1;
248				end
249				assign frame_enable = con_mul_enable == mul_delay + 7 ? 1 : 0;
250			end
251			/*
252			::description
253			Getting sine of angle.
254			*/
255			SinLUT Sin(angle, sina);
256			/*
257			::description
258			Getting cosine of angle.
259			*/
260			CosLUT Cos(angle, cosa);
261	
262			always @(posedge clk) begin
263				mul_x_a1 <= count_u - `im_width_half;
264				mul_x_a2 <= count_v - `im_height_half;
265				mul_y_a1 <= `im_width_half - count_u;
266				mul_y_a2 <= count_v - `im_height_half;
267			end
268			/*
269			::description
270			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x Signed 25bits, used for fixed multiplication. 
271			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay". 
272			All Multiplier's pipeline stage must be same, you can not change the ports' configurations!
273			*/
274			Multiplier13Sx20SRTT MulX1(.CLK(clk), .A(mul_x_a1), .B(cosa), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_x_p1));
275			/*
276			::description
277			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x Signed 25bits, used for fixed multiplication. 
278			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay". 
279			All Multiplier's pipeline stage must be same, you can not change the ports' configurations!
280			*/
281			Multiplier13Sx20SRTT MulX2(.CLK(clk), .A(mul_x_a2), .B(sina), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_x_p2));
282			/*
283			::description
284			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x Signed 25bits, used for fixed multiplication. 
285			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay". 
286			All Multiplier's pipeline stage must be same, you can not change the ports' configurations!
287			*/
288			Multiplier13Sx20SRTT MulY1(.CLK(clk), .A(mul_y_a1), .B(sina), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_y_p1));
289			/*
290			::description
291			Multiplier for Unsigned 12bits x Signed 25bits, used for fixed multiplication. 
292			You can configure the multiplier by yourself, then change the "mul_delay". 
293			All Multiplier's pipeline stage must be same, you can not change the ports' configurations!
294			*/
295			Multiplier13Sx20SRTT MulY2(.CLK(clk), .A(mul_y_a2), .B(cosa), .SCLR(~rst_n), .P(mul_y_p2));
296			/*
297			::description
298			For rounding signed fixed number.
299			*/
300			FixedRoundSigned2 #(33, 18, im_width_bits) FRSX1(clk, mul_x_p1, mul_x_r1);
301			/*
302			::description
303			For rounding signed fixed number.
304			*/
305			FixedRoundSigned2 #(33, 18, im_width_bits) FRSX2(clk, mul_x_p2, mul_x_r2);
306			/*
307			::description
308			For rounding signed fixed number.
309			*/
310			FixedRoundSigned2 #(33, 18, im_width_bits) FRSY1(clk, mul_y_p1, mul_y_r1);
311			/*
312			::description
313			For rounding signed fixed number.
314			*/
315			FixedRoundSigned2 #(33, 18, im_width_bits) FRSY2(clk, mul_y_p2, mul_y_r2);
316	
317			always @(posedge clk) begin
318				add_x <= mul_x_r1 + mul_x_r2;
319				add_y <= mul_y_r1 + mul_y_r2;
320				reg_frame_out_count_x <= add_x + `im_width_half;
321				reg_frame_out_count_y <= add_y + `im_height_half;
322			end
323			assign frame_out_count_x = reg_frame_out_count_x[im_width_bits - 1 : 0];
324			assign frame_out_count_y = reg_frame_out_count_y[im_width_bits - 1 : 0];
325	
326			if(work_mode == 0) begin
327				genvar i;
328				for (i = 0; i < ram_RL - 1; i = i + 1) begin : buffer
329					reg tmp_t, tmp_b, tmp_l, tmp_r;
330					if(i == 0) begin
331						always @(posedge clk) begin
332							tmp_t <= reg_frame_out_count_x[im_width_bits + 1] == 0 ? 1 : 0;
333							tmp_b <= reg_frame_out_count_x < im_width ? 1 : 0;
334							tmp_l <= reg_frame_out_count_y[im_width_bits + 1] == 0 ? 1 : 0;
335							tmp_r <= reg_frame_out_count_y < im_height ? 1 : 0;
336						end
337					end else begin 
338						always @(posedge clk) begin
339							tmp_t <= buffer[i - 1].tmp_t;
340							tmp_b <= buffer[i - 1].tmp_b;
341							tmp_l <= buffer[i - 1].tmp_l;
342							tmp_r <= buffer[i - 1].tmp_r;
343						end
344					end
345				end
346				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge frame_in_ready) begin
347					if(~rst_n || ~frame_in_ready) begin
348						reg_out_ready <= 0;
349						in_range_t <= 0;
350						in_range_b <= 0;
351						in_range_l <= 0;
352						in_range_r <= 0;
353						reg_out_data <= 0;
354					end else begin
355						reg_out_ready <= 1;
356						in_range_t <= buffer[ram_RL - 2].tmp_t;
357						in_range_b <= buffer[ram_RL - 2].tmp_b;
358						in_range_l <= buffer[ram_RL - 2].tmp_l;
359						in_range_r <= buffer[ram_RL - 2].tmp_r;
360						reg_out_data <= frame_in_data;
361					end
362				end
363			end else begin 
364				reg tmp_t, tmp_b, tmp_l, tmp_r;
365				always @(posedge clk) begin 
366					tmp_t <= reg_frame_out_count_x[im_width_bits + 1] == 0 ? 1 : 0;
367					tmp_b <= reg_frame_out_count_x < im_width ? 1 : 0;
368					tmp_l <= reg_frame_out_count_y[im_width_bits + 1] == 0 ? 1 : 0;
369					tmp_r <= reg_frame_out_count_y < im_height ? 1 : 0;
370				end
371				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge frame_in_ready) begin
372					if(~rst_n || ~frame_in_ready) begin
373						reg_out_ready <= 0;
374						reg_out_data <= 0;
375						in_range_t <= 0;
376						in_range_b <= 0;
377						in_range_l <= 0;
378						in_range_r <= 0;
379					end else begin
380						reg_out_ready <= 1;
381						reg_out_data <= frame_in_data;
382						in_range_t <= tmp_t;
383						in_range_b <= tmp_b;
384						in_range_l <= tmp_l;
385						in_range_r <= tmp_r;
386					end
387				end
388			end
389			assign out_ready = reg_out_ready;
390			assign out_data = out_ready & in_range_t & in_range_b & in_range_l & in_range_r ? reg_out_data : 0;
391	
392		endgenerate
393	
394	endmodule
395	`undef im_width_half
396	`undef im_height_half

%s%s
%.2f%.2f
" 48 return res 49 50 FileAll = {} 51 for root, dirs, files in os.walk('./'): 52 for f in files: 53 name, ex=os.path.splitext(f) 54 if ex in FileFormat: 55 tmp_h = re.match(r'(.*)-reqack-hdlfun', name) 56 tmp_s = re.match(r'(.*)-soft', name) 57 img = None 58 if not tmp_h and not tmp_s: 59 continue 60 if tmp_h: 61 img = tmp_h.group(1) 62 if tmp_s: 63 img = tmp_s.group(1) 64 if img not in FileAll: 65 FileAll[img] = [] 66 FileAll[img].append(root + name + ex) 67 fo = open('compare_report.txt', 'w') 68 fo.write(compare(FileAll)) 69 fo.close() 70 fo = open('compare_report_table.txt', 'w') 71 fo.write(compare_table(FileAll)) 72 fo.close()

__author__ = 'Tianyu Dai (dtysky)' from PIL import Image, ImageChops, ImageStat import os, json, re, math FileFormat = ['.jpg', '.bmp'] def name_format(root, name, ex, conf): return '%s-%s' % (name, conf['lm_gain']) def get_psnr(FileAll): psnrs = {} for name, f_pair in FileAll.items(): diffs = ImageChops.difference(\ Image.open(f_pair[0]), Image.open(f_pair[1])) stat = ImageStat.Stat(diffs) rms = sum(stat.rms) / len(stat.rms) psnr = 20.0 * math.log10(255.0 / rms) if rms != 0 else 1000*1000 psnrs[name] = psnr return psnrs def compare(FileAll): psnrs = get_psnr(FileAll) psnr_t = 0 res = 'PSNR:\n\n' for name in sorted(psnrs): psnr_t += psnrs[name] res += '%s : %f \n' % (name, psnrs[name]) res += 'Totle : %f ' % (psnr_t / len(psnrs)) return res def compare_table(FileAll): psnrs = get_psnr(FileAll) psnr_t = 0 res = '''#### PSNR:\n\n\n''' res += '\t\n' for name in sorted(psnrs): psnr_t += psnrs[name] res += '\t\t\n' % name res += '\t\t\n' % 'Total' res += '\t\n' res += '\t\n' for name in sorted(psnrs): res += '\t\t\n' % psnrs[name] res += '\t\t\n' % (psnr_t / len(psnrs)) res += '\t\n' res += "
%s%s
%.2f%.2f
" return res FileAll = {} for root, dirs, files in os.walk('./'): for f in files: name, ex=os.path.splitext(f) if ex in FileFormat: tmp_h = re.match(r'(.*)-reqack-hdlfun', name) tmp_s = re.match(r'(.*)-soft', name) img = None if not tmp_h and not tmp_s: continue if tmp_h: img = tmp_h.group(1) if tmp_s: img = tmp_s.group(1) if img not in FileAll: FileAll[img] = [] FileAll[img].append(root + name + ex) fo = open('compare_report.txt', 'w') fo.write(compare(FileAll)) fo.close() fo = open('compare_report_table.txt', 'w') fo.write(compare_table(FileAll)) fo.close()

content.thinking:
Now I have a very thorough understanding. Let me consolidate the key concepts:

**Rotate** demonstrates the random-access geometry pattern:
- Uses SinLUT/CosLUT (lookup tables, generated by sin_cos_gen.py)
- Computes inverse mapping: for each output pixel (count_u, count_v), compute source coords via rotation matrix using signed fixed-point multipliers (Multiplier13Sx20SRTT)
- FixedRoundSigned2 for rounding
- Drives FrameController (frame_enable, frame_out_count_x/y) to read source pixel from RAM
- Range check (in_range_t/b/l/r) to mask out-of-bounds

**Graying**: RGB→Gray using 3 constant-coefficient multipliers (0.299, 0.587, 0.114), pipelined sum.

**compare.py**: Computes PSNR between software result (`*-soft.bmp`) and HDL result (`*-reqack-hdlfun.bmp`), writes `compare_report.txt` and table. So convert.py converts `.res` → `*-reqack-hdlfun.bmp`.

I have enough understanding now. Let me read a few more quick ones to round out the curriculum: 
- CountGenerator.v (simple counter)
- VGA640x480.v (InOut display)
- BoardInit_AXI_S00_AXI.v (AXI register aggregation - but might be long)
- component.xml + xgui tcl (IP packaging)
- build.tcl + conf.json

Let me read these to confirm the InOut, IP packaging, and on-board build. Let me batch the smaller ones. I'll be mindful of budget ($2.47 left).

Let me read: CountGenerator.v, VGA640x480.v, a component.xml (ColorReversal), the xgui tcl, conf.json (ColorReversal), build.tcl (Point). And the BoardInit_AXI_S00_AXI.v with a line limit since it may be large.

1	//Com2DocHDL
2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	FrameController
8	
9	:Function
10	For controlling a BlockRAM from xilinx.
11	Give the first output after ram_read_latency cycles while the input enable.
12	
13	:Module
14	Main module
15	
16	:Version
17	1.0
18	
19	:Modified
20	2015-05-12
21	
22	Copyright (C) 2015  Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>
23	
24	This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
25	modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
26	License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
27	version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
28	
29	This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
30	but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
31	MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
32	Lesser General Public License for more details.
33	
34	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
35	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
36	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
37	
38	Homepage for this project:
39		http://fil.dtysky.moe
40	
41	Sources for this project:
42		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
43	
44	My e-mail:
45		dtysky@outlook.com
46	
47	My blog:
48		http://dtysky.moe
49	
50	*/
51	
52	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
53	
54	
55	module FrameController(
56		clk,
57		rst_n,
58		in_enable,
59		in_data,
60		out_ready,
61		out_data,
62		ram_addr);
63	
64		/*
65		::description
66		This module's working mode.
67		::range
68		0 for Pipline, 1 for Req-ack
69		*/
70		parameter work_mode = 0;
71		/*
72		::description
73		This module's WR mode.
74		::range
75		0 for Write, 1 for Read
76		*/
77		parameter wr_mode = 0;
78		/*
79		::description
80		Color's bit width.
81		::range
82		1 - 12
83		*/
84		parameter color_width = 8;
85		/*
86		::description
87		Width of image.
88		::range
89		1 - 4096
90		*/
91		parameter im_width = 320;
92		/*
93		::description
94		Height of image.
95		::range
96		1 - 4096
97		*/
98		parameter im_height = 240;
99		/*
100		::description
101		Address bit width of a ram for storing this image.
102		::range
103		Depend on im_width and im_height.
104		*/
105		parameter addr_width = 17;
106		/*
107		::description
108		RL of RAM, in xilinx 7-series device, it is 2.
109		::range
110		0 - 15, Depend on your using ram.
111		*/
112		parameter ram_read_latency = 2;
113		/*
114		::description
115		The first row you want to storing, used for eliminating offset.
116		::range
117		Depend on your input offset.
118		*/
119		parameter row_init = 0;
120		/*
121		::description
122		Clock.
123		*/
124		input clk;
125		/*
126		::description
127		Reset, active low.
128		*/
129		input rst_n;
130		/*
131		::description
132		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be really changes.
133		*/
134		input in_enable;
135		/*
136		::description
137		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
138		*/
139		input [color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
140		/*
141		::description
142		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
143		*/
144		output out_ready;
145		/*
146		::description
147		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
148		*/
149		output[color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
150		/*
151		::description
152		Address for ram.
153		*/
154		output[addr_width - 1 : 0] ram_addr;
155	
156		reg[addr_width - 1 : 0] reg_ram_addr;
157		reg[3 : 0] con_ready;
158	
159		assign ram_addr = reg_ram_addr;
160		assign out_data = out_ready ? in_data : 0;
161	
162		generate
163	
164			if(wr_mode == 0) begin
165	
166				if(work_mode == 0) begin
167					always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
168						if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
169							reg_ram_addr <= row_init * im_width;
170						else if(reg_ram_addr == im_width * im_height - 1)
171							reg_ram_addr <= 0;
172						else
173							reg_ram_addr <= reg_ram_addr + 1;
174					end
175				end else begin 
176					always @(posedge in_enable or negedge rst_n) begin
177						if(~rst_n)
178							reg_ram_addr <= row_init * im_width - 1;
179						else if(reg_ram_addr == im_width * im_height - 1)
180							reg_ram_addr <= 0;
181						else
182							reg_ram_addr <= reg_ram_addr + 1;
183					end
184				end
185				assign out_ready = ~rst_n || ~in_enable ? 0 : 1;
186	
187			end else begin
188	
189				if(work_mode == 0) begin
190					always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
191						if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
192							reg_ram_addr <= 0;
193						else if(reg_ram_addr == im_width * im_height - 1)
194							reg_ram_addr <= 0;
195						else
196							reg_ram_addr <= reg_ram_addr + 1;
197					end
198				end else begin 
199					always @(posedge in_enable or negedge rst_n) begin
200						if(~rst_n)
201							reg_ram_addr <= 0 - 1;
202						else if(reg_ram_addr == im_width * im_height - 1)
203							reg_ram_addr <= 0;
204						else
205							reg_ram_addr <= reg_ram_addr + 1;
206					end
207				end
208	
209				always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
210					if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
211						con_ready <= 0;
212					else if (con_ready == ram_read_latency)
213						con_ready <= con_ready;
214					else
215						con_ready <= con_ready + 1;
216				end
217				assign out_ready = con_ready == ram_read_latency ? 1 : 0;
218	
219			end
220	
221		endgenerate
222	
223	endmodule

1	//Com2DocHDL
2	/*
3	:Project
4	FPGA-Imaging-Library
5	
6	:Design
7	RankFilter
8	
9	:Function
10	Local filter - Rank filter, it always used for denoising with preserving edge. 
11	It will give the first output after sum_stage + 2 cycles while the input enable. 
12	
13	:Module
14	Main module
15	
16	:Version
17	1.0
18	
19	:Modified
20	2015-05-21
21	
22	Copyright (C) 2015 Tianyu Dai (dtysky) <dtysky@outlook.com>
23	
24	This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
25	modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
26	License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
27	version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
28	
29	This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
30	but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
31	MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
32	Lesser General Public License for more details.
33	
34	You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public
35	License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software
36	Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301  USA
37	
38	Homepage for this project:
39		http://fil.dtysky.moe
40	
41	Sources for this project:
42		https://github.com/dtysky/FPGA-Imaging-Library
43	
44	My e-mail:
45		dtysky@outlook.com
46	
47	My blog:
48		http://dtysky.moe
49	
50	*/
51	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
52	`define full_win_width window_width * window_width
53	
54	module RankFifter(
55		clk, 
56		rst_n, 
57		rank, 
58		in_enable, 
59		in_data, 
60		out_ready, 
61		out_data);
62	
63		/*
64		::description
65		This module's working mode.
66		::range
67		0 for Pipeline, 1 for Req-ack
68		*/
69		parameter[0 : 0] work_mode = 0;
70		/*
71		::description
72		The width(and height) of window.
73		::range
74		2 - 15
75		*/
76		parameter[3 : 0] window_width = 3;
77		/*
78		::description
79		Color's bit width.
80		::range
81		1 - 12
82		*/
83		parameter[3 : 0] color_width = 8;
84		/*
85		::description
86		Stage of sum.
87		::range
88		Depend on width of window, int(log8(window_width^2)) + 1
89		*/
90		parameter[2 : 0] sum_stage = 2;
91		/*
92		::description
93		Width bits of full size of window.
94		::range
95		Depend on width of window
96		*/
97		parameter full_win_bits = 4;
98		/*
99		::description
100		Clock.
101		*/
102		input clk;
103		/*
104		::description
105		Reset, active low.
106		*/
107		input rst_n;
108		/*
109		::description
110		Filter's rank, if half of full size of window, this module working as median filter, etc.
111		*/
112		input[full_win_bits - 1 : 0] rank;
113		/*
114		::description
115		Input data enable, in pipeline mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be really changes.
116		*/
117		input in_enable;
118		/*
119		::description
120		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
121		*/
122		input [color_width * window_width * window_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
123		/*
124		::description
125		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
126		*/
127		output out_ready;
128		/*
129		::description
130		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
131		*/
132		output[color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
133		
134		reg[color_width - 1 : 0] reg_in_data[0 : `full_win_width - 1];
135		reg[`full_win_width - 1 : 0] big_flag[0 : `full_win_width - 1];
136		wire[`full_win_width - 1 : 0] rank_list;
137		wire[full_win_bits - 1 : 0] rank_res;
138		reg[3 : 0] con_enable;
139		reg[color_width - 1 : 0] reg_out_data;
140	
141		genvar i, j, k;
142		generate
143	
144			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
145				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable) 
146					con_enable <= 0;
147				else if(con_enable == sum_stage + 3)
148					con_enable <= con_enable;
149				else
150					con_enable <= con_enable + 1;
151			end
152			assign out_ready = con_enable == sum_stage + 3 ? 1 : 0;
153	
154			for (i = 0; i < `full_win_width; i = i + 1) begin
155				if(work_mode == 0) begin
156					always @(*)
157						reg_in_data[i] = in_data[(i + 1) * color_width - 1 : i * color_width];
158				end else begin
159					always @(posedge in_enable)
160						reg_in_data[i] = in_data[(i + 1) * color_width - 1 : i * color_width];
161				end
162			end
163	
164			if(work_mode == 0) begin : pipemode
165				for (i = 0; i < sum_stage + 2; i = i + 1) begin : buffer
166					reg[color_width - 1 : 0] b[0 : `full_win_width - 1];
167					for (j = 0; j < `full_win_width; j = j + 1) begin
168						if(i == 0) begin
169							always @(posedge clk)
170								b[j] <= reg_in_data[j];
171						end else begin
172							always @(posedge clk)
173								b[j] <= buffer[i - 1].b[j];
174						end
175					end
176				end
177			end
178	
179			for (i = 0; i < `full_win_width; i = i + 1) begin
180				always @(posedge clk)
181					big_flag[i][i] <= 0;
182			end
183	
184			for (i = 0; i < `full_win_width; i = i + 1) begin
185				for (j = i + 1; j < `full_win_width; j = j + 1) begin
186					always @(posedge clk) begin
187						if(reg_in_data[i] >= reg_in_data[j]) begin
188							big_flag[i][j] <= 1;
189							big_flag[j][i] <= 0;
190						end else begin 
191							big_flag[i][j] <= 0;
192							big_flag[j][i] <= 1;
193						end
194					end
195				end
196			end
197	
198			for (i = 0; i < sum_stage; i = i + 1) begin : pipe
199				for (k = 0; k < `full_win_width; k = k + 1) begin : pixel
200					reg[((`full_win_width - 1) >> 3 * (sum_stage - i)) + 3 : 0] sum[0 : (`full_win_width - 1) >> 3 * (i + 1)];
201					for (j = 0; j < ((`full_win_width - 1) >> 3 * (i + 1)) + 1; j = j + 1) begin
202						if(i == 0) begin
203							if(`full_win_width - j * 8 >= 8) begin
204								always @(posedge clk)
205									sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 1] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 2] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 3] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 4] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 5] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 6] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 7];
206							end else begin
207								case (`full_win_width % 8)
208									1 : always @(posedge clk)
209											sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0];
210									2 : always @(posedge clk)
211											sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 1];
212									3 : always @(posedge clk)
213											sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 1] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 2];
214									4 : always @(posedge clk)
215											sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 1] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 2] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 3];
216									5 : always @(posedge clk)
217											sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 1] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 2] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 3] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 4];
218									6 : always @(posedge clk)
219											sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 1] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 2] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 3] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 4] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 5];
220									7 : always @(posedge clk)
221											sum[j] <= big_flag[k][8 * j + 0] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 1] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 2] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 3] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 4] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 5] + big_flag[k][8 * j + 6];
222									default : /* default */;
223								endcase
224							end
225						end else begin 
226							if((`full_win_width >> 3 * i) - j * 8 >= 8) begin
227								always @(posedge clk)
228									sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 1] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 2] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 3] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 4] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 5] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 6] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 7];
229							end else begin
230								case ((`full_win_width >> 3 * i) % 8 + 1)
231									1 : always @(posedge clk)
232											sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0];
233									2 : always @(posedge clk)
234											sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 1];
235									3 : always @(posedge clk)
236											sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 1] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 2];
237									4 : always @(posedge clk)
238											sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 1] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 2] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 3];
239									5 : always @(posedge clk)
240											sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 1] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 2] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 3] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 4];
241									6 : always @(posedge clk)
242											sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 1] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 2] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 3] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 4] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 5];
243									7 : always @(posedge clk)
244											sum[j] <= pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 0] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 1] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 2] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 3] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 4] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 5] + pipe[i - 1].pixel[k].sum[8 * j + 6];
245									default : /* default */;
246								endcase
247							end
248						end
249					end
250				end
251			end
252	
253			for (i = 0; i < `full_win_width; i = i + 1) begin
254				assign rank_list[i] = pipe[sum_stage - 1].pixel[i].sum[0] == rank ? 1 : 0;
255			end
256			Encoder #(window_width, full_win_bits) ED(rank_list, rank_res);
257	
258			if(work_mode == 0) begin
259				always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= pipemode.buffer[sum_stage + 1].b[rank_res];
260			end else begin 
261				always @(posedge clk) reg_out_data <= reg_in_data[rank_res];
262			end
263			assign out_data = out_ready ? reg_out_data : 0;
264	
265		endgenerate
266	endmodule
267	`undef full_win_width

1	################################################################
2	# This is a generated script based on design: design_1
3	#
4	# Though there are limitations about the generated script,
5	# the main purpose of this utility is to make learning
6	set prj_name "Geometry"
7	set prj_path "Proj/"
8	
9	create_project $prj_name $prj_path -part xc7z010clg400-1 -force
10	
11	set_property ip_repo_paths {../../BoardInit_AXI ../../Connector ../../Generator ../../Geometry ../../InOut ../../LocalFilter ../../Point} [current_fileset]
12	update_ip_catalog
13	
14	# IP Integrator Tcl commands easier.
15	################################################################
16	
17	################################################################
18	# START
19	################################################################
20	
21	# To test this script, run the following commands from Vivado Tcl console:
22	# source design_1_script.tcl
23	
24	# If you do not already have a project created,
25	# you can create a project using the following command:
26	#    create_project project_1 myproj -part xc7z010clg400-1
27	
28	
29	# CHANGE DESIGN NAME HERE
30	set design_name design_1
31	
32	# If you do not already have an existing IP Integrator design open,
33	# you can create a design using the following command:
34	#    create_bd_design $design_name
35	
36	# CHECKING IF PROJECT EXISTS
37	if { [get_projects -quiet] eq "" } {
38	   puts "ERROR: Please open or create a project!"
39	   return 1
40	}
41	
42	
43	# Creating design if needed
44	set errMsg ""
45	set nRet 0
46	
47	set cur_design [current_bd_design -quiet]
48	set list_cells [get_bd_cells -quiet]
49	
50	if { ${design_name} eq "" } {
51	   # USE CASES:
52	   #    1) Design_name not set
53	
54	   set errMsg "ERROR: Please set the variable <design_name> to a non-empty value."
55	   set nRet 1
56	
57	} elseif { ${cur_design} ne "" && ${list_cells} eq "" } {
58	   # USE CASES:
59	   #    2): Current design opened AND is empty AND names same.
60	   #    3): Current design opened AND is empty AND names diff; design_name NOT in project.
61	   #    4): Current design opened AND is empty AND names diff; design_name exists in project.
62	
63	   if { $cur_design ne $design_name } {
64	      puts "INFO: Changing value of <design_name> from <$design_name> to <$cur_design> since current design is empty."
65	      set design_name [get_property NAME $cur_design]
66	   }
67	   puts "INFO: Constructing design in IPI design <$cur_design>..."
68	
69	} elseif { ${cur_design} ne "" && $list_cells ne "" && $cur_design eq $design_name } {
70	   # USE CASES:
71	   #    5) Current design opened AND has components AND same names.
72	
73	   set errMsg "ERROR: Design <$design_name> already exists in your project, please set the variable <design_name> to another value."
74	   set nRet 1
75	} elseif { [get_files -quiet ${design_name}.bd] ne "" } {
76	   # USE CASES: 
77	   #    6) Current opened design, has components, but diff names, design_name exists in project.
78	   #    7) No opened design, design_name exists in project.
79	
80	   set errMsg "ERROR: Design <$design_name> already exists in your project, please set the variable <design_name> to another value."
81	   set nRet 2
82	
83	} else {
84	   # USE CASES:
85	   #    8) No opened design, design_name not in project.
86	   #    9) Current opened design, has components, but diff names, design_name not in project.
87	
88	   puts "INFO: Currently there is no design <$design_name> in project, so creating one..."
89	
90	   create_bd_design $design_name
91	
92	   puts "INFO: Making design <$design_name> as current_bd_design."
93	   current_bd_design $design_name
94	
95	}
96	
97	puts "INFO: Currently the variable <design_name> is equal to \"$design_name\"."
98	
99	if { $nRet != 0 } {
100	   puts $errMsg
101	   return $nRet
102	}

104	##################################################################
105	# DESIGN PROCs
106	##################################################################
107	
108	
109	
110	# Procedure to create entire design; Provide argument to make
111	# procedure reusable. If parentCell is "", will use root.
112	proc create_root_design { parentCell } {
113	
114	  if { $parentCell eq "" } {
115	     set parentCell [get_bd_cells /]
116	  }
117	
118	  # Get object for parentCell
119	  set parentObj [get_bd_cells $parentCell]
120	  if { $parentObj == "" } {
121	     puts "ERROR: Unable to find parent cell <$parentCell>!"
122	     return
123	  }
124	
125	  # Make sure parentObj is hier blk
126	  set parentType [get_property TYPE $parentObj]
127	  if { $parentType ne "hier" } {
128	     puts "ERROR: Parent <$parentObj> has TYPE = <$parentType>. Expected to be <hier>."
129	     return
130	  }
131	
132	  # Save current instance; Restore later
133	  set oldCurInst [current_bd_instance .]
134	
135	  # Set parent object as current
136	  current_bd_instance $parentObj
137	
138

151	  set vga_red [ create_bd_port -dir O -from 4 -to 0 vga_red ]
152	  set vga_vsync [ create_bd_port -dir O vga_vsync ]

################################################################
# This is a generated script based on design: design_1
#
# Though there are limitations about the generated script,
# the main purpose of this utility is to make learning
set prj_name "Geometry"
set prj_path "Proj/"

create_project $prj_name $prj_path -part xc7z010clg400-1 -force

set_property ip_repo_paths {../../BoardInit_AXI ../../Connector ../../Generator ../../Geometry ../../InOut ../../LocalFilter ../../Point} [current_fileset]
update_ip_catalog

# IP Integrator Tcl commands easier.
################################################################

################################################################
# START
################################################################

# To test this script, run the following commands from Vivado Tcl console:
# source design_1_script.tcl

# If you do not already have a project created,
# you can create a project using the following command:
#    create_project project_1 myproj -part xc7z010clg400-1


# CHANGE DESIGN NAME HERE
set design_name design_1

# If you do not already have an existing IP Integrator design open,
# you can create a design using the following command:
#    create_bd_design $design_name

# CHECKING IF PROJECT EXISTS
if { [get_projects -quiet] eq "" } {
   puts "ERROR: Please open or create a project!"
   return 1
}


# Creating design if needed
set errMsg ""
set nRet 0

set cur_design [current_bd_design -quiet]
set list_cells [get_bd_cells -quiet]

if { ${design_name} eq "" } {
   # USE CASES:
   #    1) Design_name not set

   set errMsg "ERROR: Please set the variable <design_name> to a non-empty value."
   set nRet 1

} elseif { ${cur_design} ne "" && ${list_cells} eq "" } {
   # USE CASES:
   #    2): Current design opened AND is empty AND names same.
   #    3): Current design opened AND is empty AND names diff; design_name NOT in project.
   #    4): Current design opened AND is empty AND names diff; design_name exists in project.

   if { $cur_design ne $design_name } {
      puts "INFO: Changing value of <design_name> from <$design_name> to <$cur_design> since current design is empty."
      set design_name [get_property NAME $cur_design]
   }
   puts "INFO: Constructing design in IPI design <$cur_design>..."

} elseif { ${cur_design} ne "" && $list_cells ne "" && $cur_design eq $design_name } {
   # USE CASES:
   #    5) Current design opened AND has components AND same names.

   set errMsg "ERROR: Design <$design_name> already exists in your project, please set the variable <design_name> to another value."
   set nRet 1
} elseif { [get_files -quiet ${design_name}.bd] ne "" } {
   # USE CASES: 
   #    6) Current opened design, has components, but diff names, design_name exists in project.
   #    7) No opened design, design_name exists in project.

   set errMsg "ERROR: Design <$design_name> already exists in your project, please set the variable <design_name> to another value."
   set nRet 2

} else {
   # USE CASES:
   #    8) No opened design, design_name not in project.
   #    9) Current opened design, has components, but diff names, design_name not in project.

   puts "INFO: Currently there is no design <$design_name> in project, so creating one..."

   create_bd_design $design_name

   puts "INFO: Making design <$design_name> as current_bd_design."
   current_bd_design $design_name

}

puts "INFO: Currently the variable <design_name> is equal to \"$design_name\"."

if { $nRet != 0 } {
   puts $errMsg
   return $nRet
}

##################################################################
# DESIGN PROCs
##################################################################



# Procedure to create entire design; Provide argument to make
# procedure reusable. If parentCell is "", will use root.
proc create_root_design { parentCell } {

  if { $parentCell eq "" } {
     set parentCell [get_bd_cells /]
  }

  # Get object for parentCell
  set parentObj [get_bd_cells $parentCell]
  if { $parentObj == "" } {
     puts "ERROR: Unable to find parent cell <$parentCell>!"
     return
  }

  # Make sure parentObj is hier blk
  set parentType [get_property TYPE $parentObj]
  if { $parentType ne "hier" } {
     puts "ERROR: Parent <$parentObj> has TYPE = <$parentType>. Expected to be <hier>."
     return
  }

  # Save current instance; Restore later
  set oldCurInst [current_bd_instance .]

  # Set parent object as current
  current_bd_instance $parentObj


  # Create interface ports

  # Create ports
  set I2C_SCLK [ create_bd_port -dir O I2C_SCLK ]
  set I2C_SDAT [ create_bd_port -dir IO I2C_SDAT ]

  set vga_hsync [ create_bd_port -dir O vga_hsync ]
  set vga_red [ create_bd_port -dir O -from 4 -to 0 vga_red ]
  set vga_vsync [ create_bd_port -dir O vga_vsync ]
  set vsync [ create_bd_port -dir I vsync ]
  set xclk [ create_bd_port -dir O xclk ]

  # Create instance: BoardInit_AXI_0, and set properties
  set BoardInit_AXI_0 [ create_bd_cell -type ip -vlnv dtysky.moe:F-I-L:BoardInit_AXI:1.0 BoardInit_AXI_0 ]

  # Create instance: Bram8x320x240_0, and set properties
  set Bram8x320x240_0 [ create_bd_cell -type ip -vlnv xilinx.com:ip:Bram8x320x240:1.0 Bram8x320x240_0 ]

  set Bram8x320x240_1 [ create_bd_cell -type ip -vlnv xilinx.com:ip:Bram8x320x240:1.0 Bram8x320x240_1 ]

  set ColorBin2Channels_0 [ create_bd_cell -type ip -vlnv dtysky.moe:F-I-L:ColorBin2Channels:1.0 ColorBin2Channels_0 ]
  set_property -dict [ list CONFIG.color_channels {1}  ] $ColorBin2Channels_0

  # Create instance: ColorGray2Channels_0, and set properties
  set ColorGray2Channels_0 [ create_bd_cell -type ip -vlnv dtysky.moe:F-I-L:ColorGray2Channels:1.0 ColorGray2Channels_0 ]

  set ColorRGB16toRGB24_0 [ create_bd_cell -type ip -vlnv dtysky.moe:F-I-L:ColorRGB16toRGB24:1.0 ColorRGB16toRGB24_0 ]

  set ColorReversal_0 [ create_bd_cell -type ip -vlnv dtysky.moe:F-I-L:ColorReversal:1.0 ColorReversal_0 ]

  # Restore current instance
  current_bd_instance $oldCurInst

  save_bd_design
}

##################################################################
# MAIN FLOW
##################################################################

create_root_design ""


generate_target {synthesis implementation} [get_files $prj_path/$prj_name.srcs/sources_1/bd/design_1/design_1.bd]

open_run impl_1

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete understanding of the full system, including the on-board block design (BD) flow:

**On-board architecture (TestOnBoard):**
- Zynq PS (processing_system7) → AXI interconnect → BoardInit_AXI (the AXI4-Lite register aggregation IP)
- BoardInit_AXI exposes registers: th_mode, th1, th2, ct_scale, lm_gain, sels, rst_all_n
- Camera (OV7670) → camCap → ColorRGB16toRGB24 → Graying → Bram8x320x240 (frame buffer write via FrameController_1 wr_mode=0)
- FrameController_0 (wr_mode=1, read) reads from Bram → feeds parallel point IPs (ColorReversal, Threshold, LightnessTransform, ContrastTransform)
- Mux4 selects which point op output to write back to a second Bram (Bram8x320x240_1) via FrameController_1
- VGA640x480 reads from Bram8x320x240_1 → VGA display
- The whole dataflow: camera capture → RGB→gray → frame buffer → read out → point op → mux → frame buffer → VGA display

**FrameController (original):** linear address counter (addr++ wrapping at im_width*im_height), for sequential stream access to a frame buffer (write one frame, read back). wr_mode 0=write, 1=read.

**FrameController2:** computes addr = y*im_width + x via multiplier, for random 2D access (needed by Rotate/Scale/Shear inverse mapping).

**RankFilter:** ranking via pairwise comparison matrix (big_flag), then tree-reduction of bit sums to find the element whose rank matches, then Encoder to index. Median filter when rank = half.

I now have enough to design a comprehensive curriculum. Let me also quickly verify the existence of a few files I want to reference (the gtcl, xgui tcl, convert.py, FrameController README, sin_cos_gen.py, Encoder.v, FixedRoundSigned.v, DataWidthConvert.v, ErosionDilationBin, MatchTemplateBin, Shear, Scale, Mirror, Pan, ContrastTransform, LightnessTransform, ThresholdLocal, the BoardInit_AXI driver .h, the S00_AXI wrapper). I've seen most exist via git ls-files. Let me confirm a few I haven't directly seen the content of but want to reference: I'll trust git ls-files for existence. I've confirmed these exist:
- Connector/DataWidthConvert/srcs/DataWidthConvert.v ✓
- Geometry/Rotate/.../SinLUT.v, CosLUT.v, FixedRoundSigned2.v, sin_cos_gen.py ✓
- LocalFilter/RankFilter/.../Encoder.v, generate.py ✓
- BoardInit_AXI/hdl/BoardInit_AXI_v1_0_S00_AXI.v, drivers/.../BoardInit_AXI.h, BoardInit_AXI.c ✓
- TestOnBoard/Point/ForBuild/Constraints.xdc ✓
- Point/ContrastTransform, LightnessTransform ✓
- Geometry/Mirror, Pan, Scale, Shear ✓
- LocalFilter/ErosionDilationBin, MatchTemplateBin, ThresholdLocal ✓
- xgui/*.tcl, gui/*.gtcl ✓

I should quickly read the BoardInit_AXI_S00_AXI.v (to understand the register map / AXI pattern) and the .h driver, plus a couple I haven't seen: DataWidthConvert, SinLUT, FixedRoundSigned, convert.py, the gtcl/xgui tcl. Let me read a focused batch to be accurate about the AXI register aggregation and the convert step, and the LUT generation. I'll limit the AXI read.

Actually, I have strong enough understanding of the AXI pattern from Main.c (the driver API: BOARDINIT_Init, SetWithCheck, SetFixed, Reset, and the field struct bdit.ThMode/Th1/Th2/CtScale/LmGain/Sels). And the BD shows the BoardInit_AXI outputs. Let me read the driver .h to get the exact register struct, and convert.py, and the S00_AXI wrapper header portion. And SinLUT + FixedRoundSigned2 + DataWidthConvert + the gtcl. Let me batch a final set.

Let me be economical: read BoardInit_AXI.h, convert.py (ColorReversal), DataWidthConvert.v, SinLUT.v, and the S00_AXI.v (limited to first ~80 lines for the register map). And one xgui tcl + gtcl to describe Com2DocHDL. That's 7 reads. Budget ~$2.3 left, fine.

content.text: 我已经掌握了完整的板级模块设计流程（Camera → RGB24 → Gray → frame buffer → point ops → mux → frame buffer → VGA，由 BoardInit_AXI AXI4-Lite 寄存器驱动）。让我最后阅读一批内容，以确认 AXI 驱动程序、LUT/fixed-point 辅助工具、转换器步骤以及 Com2DocHDL GUI 脚本。

/****************** Include Files ********************/
#include "xil_types.h"

#define BOARDINIT_RstN BOARDINIT_AXI_S00_AXI_SLV_REG0_OFFSET

#define BOARDINIT_CtScale BOARDINIT_AXI_S00_AXI_SLV_REG4_OFFSET
#define BOARDINIT_LmGain BOARDINIT_AXI_S00_AXI_SLV_REG5_OFFSET
#define BOARDINIT_Rank BOARDINIT_AXI_S00_AXI_SLV_REG6_OFFSET
#define BOARDINIT_EdMode BOARDINIT_AXI_S00_AXI_SLV_REG7_OFFSET

/**************************** Type Definitions *****************************/
/**
 *
 * Write a value to a BOARDINIT_AXI register. A 32 bit write is performed.
 * If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least
 * significant data is written.
 *
 * @param   BaseAddress is the base address of the BOARDINIT_AXIdevice.
 * @param   RegOffset is the register offset from the base to write to.
 * @param   Data is the data written to the register.
 *
 * @return  None.
 *
 * @note
 * C-style signature:
 * 	void BOARDINIT_AXI_mWriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, unsigned RegOffset, u32 Data)
 *
 */



typedef struct
{
	u32 BaseAddress;
	u32 RstN;
	u32 ThMode;
	u32 Th1;
	u32 Th2;
	u32 CtScale;
	u32 LmGain;
	u32 Rank;
	u32 EdMode;
	u32 EdTemplate;
	u32 MtTemplate;
	u32 CropTop;
	u32 CropBottom;
	u32 CropLeft;
	u32 CropRight;
	u32 MirrorMode;
	u32 OffsetX;
	u32 OffsetY;
	u32 ScaleX;
	u32 ScaleY;
	u32 ShU;
	u32 ShV;
	u32 Angle;
	u32 Sels;
} BOARDINIT;

void BOARDINIT_Init(BOARDINIT *bdit, u32 BaseAddress);
void BOARDINIT_SetWithCheck(u32 address, u32 data);
void BOARDINIT_Reset(BOARDINIT *bdit);
u32 Format2Fixed(float num, int rel_width, int dec_width);
void BOARDINIT_SetFixed(u32 Address, float num, int rel_width, int dec_width);

#define BOARDINIT_Set(address, data) \
  	Xil_Out32(address, data)

#define BOARDINIT_Get(address) \
  	Xil_In32(address)

#define BOARDINIT_AXI_mWriteReg(BaseAddress, RegOffset, Data) \
  	Xil_Out32((BaseAddress) + (RegOffset), (u32)(Data))

/**
 *
 * Read a value from a BOARDINIT_AXI register. A 32 bit read is performed.
 * If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least
 * significant data is read from the register. The most significant data
 * will be read as 0.
 *
 * @param   BaseAddress is the base address of the BOARDINIT_AXI device.
 * @param   RegOffset is the register offset from the base to write to.
 *
 * @return  Data is the data from the register.
 *
 * @note
 * C-style signature:
 * 	u32 BOARDINIT_AXI_mReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, unsigned RegOffset)
 *
 */
#define BOARDINIT_AXI_mReadReg(BaseAddress, RegOffset) \
    Xil_In32((BaseAddress) + (RegOffset))

/************************** Function Prototypes ****************************/
/**
 *
 * Run a self-test on the driver/device. Note this may be a destructive test if
 * resets of the device are performed.
 *
 * If the hardware system is not built correctly, this function may never
 * return to the caller.
 *
 * @param   baseaddr_p is the base address of the BOARDINIT_AXI instance to be worked on.
 *
 * @return
 *
 *    - XST_SUCCESS   if all self-test code passed
 *    - XST_FAILURE   if any self-test code failed
 *
 * @note    Caching must be turned off for this function to work.
 * @note    Self test may fail if data memory and device are not on the same bus.
 *
 */
XStatus BOARDINIT_AXI_Reg_SelfTest(void * baseaddr_p);

#endif // BOARDINIT_AXI_H

3	from PIL import Image
4	import os
5	
6	FileFormat = ['.res']
7	
8	def name_format(root, name, ex):
9		return '%s-hdlfun.bmp' % name
10	
11	def convert(data):
12		mode = data[2].strip()
13		data_res = []
14		for p in data[3:]:
15			if mode == 'RGB':
16				p = p.split(' ')
17				data_res.append((int(p[0]), int(p[1]), int(p[2])))
18			else:
19				data_res.append(int(p))
20		xsize, ysize = int(data[0]), int(data[1])
21		im_res = Image.new(mode, (xsize, ysize))
22		im_res.putdata(data_res)
23		return im_res
24	
25	FileAll = []
26	for root, dirs, files in os.walk('../FunSimForHDL'):
27	    for f in files:
28	    	name, ex=os.path.splitext(f)
29	        if ex in FileFormat:
30	        	FileAll.append((root+'/', name, ex))
31	for root, name, ex in FileAll:
32		dat_src = open(root + name + ex)
33		data_src = dat_src.readlines()
34		convert(data_src).save(name_format(root, name, ex))
35		dat_src.close()

3	// Company: None

17	// Revision 0.01 - File Created
18	// Additional Comments:
19	//

21	
22	
23	module DataWidthConvert(i, o);
24	
25		parameter data_width_in = 32;
26		parameter data_width_out = 4;	
27	
28		input[data_width_in - 1 : 0] i;
29		output[data_width_out - 1 : 0] o;
30	
31		generate
32			if(data_width_in >= data_width_out) begin
33				assign o = i[data_width_in - 1 : 0];
34			end else begin 
35				assign o = {{data_width_out - data_width_in{1'b0}} ,i};
36			end
37		endgenerate
38	endmodule

content.thinking:
I now have a complete, thorough understanding of the entire project. Let me consolidate my understanding and design the curriculum.

## Complete Project Understanding

**FPGA-Imaging-Library (F-I-L)** — an open-source Verilog/SystemVerilog library of image-processing IP cores for Xilinx Vivado/Zynq. All IPs share a standardized interface and folder structure, support Pipeline and Req-ack modes, and are validated by a software-reference-vs-HDL-simulation PSNR comparison methodology.

### Standardized IP interface
- Parameters: `work_mode` (0=Pipeline, 1=Req-ack), `color_channels` (1=gray, 3=rgb), `color_width` (1–12)
- Ports: `clk`, `rst_n` (active low), `in_enable`, `in_data`, `out_ready`, `out_data`
- Reset uses `negedge rst_n` AND `negedge in_enable` (in_enable doubles as reset in pipeline mode)
- Pipeline: output on posedge clk after N cycles; Req-ack: output on posedge in_enable, data only changes when in_enable high
- `::description` / `::range` comments parsed by `Com2DocHDL` tool to generate component.xml + xgui tcl

### Standardized folder structure (per processing IP)
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/sources_1/new/<Name>.v` — main RTL
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/sim_1/new/<Name>_TB.sv` — SystemVerilog testbench (6 variants: RGB/Gray/Bin × Pipeline/ReqAck)
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/component.xml` — IP-XACT packaging
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/xgui/<Name>_v1_0.tcl` — XGUI parameter layout (generated from ::description)
- `HDL/<Name>.srcs/gui/<Name>_v1_0.gtcl` — GUI generation
- `SoftwareSim/sim.py` — Python 2.7 + PIL reference implementation
- `HDLSimDataGen/create.py` — generate .dat input stimulus from images
- `FunSimForHDL/` — ModelSim project (Run.do / RunOver.do, .mpf)
- `SimResCheck/convert.py` — convert .res → bmp; `compare.py` — PSNR vs software result
- `ImageForTest/conf.json` — test config
- `README.md` — simulation steps

### Categories
1. **Point** (per-pixel, ~1-cycle):
   - ColorReversal (bitwise NOT)
   - Threshold (gray→binary, base/contour modes)
   - Graying (RGB→gray, 3 constant-coefficient multipliers 0.299/0.587/0.114)
   - ContrastTransform (multiplier, fixed-point scale)
   - LightnessTransform (gain, signed/True2Comp)

2. **LocalFilter** (window-based neighborhood):
   - MeanFilter (adder tree + shift-divide approximation)
   - RankFilter (pairwise comparison matrix + tree reduction + Encoder; median when rank=half)
   - ErosionDilationBin (morphology)
   - MatchTemplateBin
   - ThresholdLocal (combines Mean + Rank)
   - All consume WindowGenerator output (window_width² pixels)

3. **Geometry**:
   - Streaming: Crop (range check on count_x/count_y), Mirror, Pan
   - Random-access (need frame buffer + FrameController2): Rotate (SinLUT/CosLUT + signed fixed-point multipliers + FixedRoundSigned2), Scale (unsigned fixed-point), Shear (signed fixed-point)

4. **Generator** (data feeding infrastructure):
   - CountGenerator (x/y pixel counters)
   - RowsGenerator (FIFO line buffer, rows_width rows in parallel, pipeline-only)
   - WindowGenerator (shift registers → window_width² window, both modes, input_ack in req-ack)
   - FrameController (linear RAM address counter, sequential frame buffer R/W)
   - FrameController2 (2D RAM address y*im_width+x via multiplier, random access for geometry)

5. **Connector** (data routing/format):
   - Mux2/Mux4/Mux8, Or8 (selectors)
   - DataCombin2/3, DataSplit4, DataDelay, DataWidthConvert
   - ColorBin2Channels, ColorGray2Channels, ColorRGB16toRGB24, ColorRGB24toVGA (color format converters)

6. **InOut** (hardware I/O):
   - VGA640x480 (VGA timing generator, reads frame_addr from BRAM)
   - Cam/camCap (OV7670 camera capture)
   - IIC_Ctrl (I2C config for OV7670)
   - Bram8x320x240 (block RAM frame buffer)

7. **BoardInit_AXI** (AXI4-Lite register aggregation IP):
   - `hdl/BoardInit_AXI_v1_0_S00_AXI.v` — AXI4-Lite slave with 32 registers
   - `hdl/BoardInit_AXI_v1_0.v` — top wrapper aggregating all category IPs, exposes parameter registers (ThMode, Th1, Th2, CtScale, LmGain, Rank, EdMode, CropTop/Bottom/Left/Right, MirrorMode, OffsetX/Y, ScaleX/Y, ShU/V, Angle, Sels) + rst_all_n to the PL fabric
   - C driver (`drivers/.../BoardInit_AXI.h/.c`) — BOARDINIT struct, Init/SetWithCheck/SetFixed/Reset, Format2Fixed
   - This is the bridge between Zynq PS (software) and the PL image pipeline

8. **TestOnBoard** (full Zynq system per category):
   - `build.tcl` — Vivado block design (BD) script: PS7 → AXI → BoardInit_AXI; camera → RGB24 → Graying → BRAM(frame write) → FrameController(read) → parallel point IPs → Mux4 → BRAM(frame write) → VGA
   - `ForBuild/Main.c` — PS-side C configuring registers then resetting
   - `ForBuild/Constraints.xdc` — pin constraints
   - Categories: Point, LocalFilter, Geometry

### Simulation methodology
1. conf.json → test images
2. SoftwareSim/sim.py → reference `*-soft.bmp`
3. HDLSimDataGen/create.py → `.dat` (binary pixel streams) + imgindex.dat
4. FunSimForHDL ModelSim → testbench drives DUT, writes `-pipeline.res` / `-reqack.res`
5. SimResCheck/convert.py → `*-hdlfun.bmp`
6. SimResCheck/compare.py → PSNR report (software vs HDL)

### On-board dataflow (TestOnBoard/Point build.tcl)
OV7670 camera → camCap → ColorRGB16toRGB24 → Graying → Bram8x320x240_0 (write via FrameController_0 wr_mode=0... actually FrameController_1 wr_mode=0 writes) → FrameController_0 (wr_mode=1 read) → {ColorReversal, Threshold, LightnessTransform, ContrastTransform} in parallel → Mux4_1 (data) + Mux4_0 (ready) select → Bram8x320x240_1 (write) → VGA640x480 reads → VGA display. BoardInit_AXI sets all parameters from PS.

Now let me design the curriculum. This is a medium-to-large project. I'll aim for ~22-26 lectures across 3 levels, organized into units.

## Curriculum Design

### Unit 1 (beginner): 项目入门与运行方式
- L1: 项目总览与定位 (what is F-I-L, README, categories)
- L2: 目录结构与标准化IP文件夹布局 (folder structure per IP)
- L3: 工具链与仿真运行方式 (Vivado/ModelSim/Python2.7+PIL, the 5-step sim methodology)
- L4: 标准化IP接口与两种工作模式 (work_mode, clk/rst_n/in_enable/in_data/out_ready/out_data, pipeline vs req-ack)

### Unit 2 (beginner): 第一个IP核——点运算
- L5: ColorReversal——最简单的点运算 (bitwise NOT, 1-cycle, the generate/always pattern)
- L6: Threshold——灰度转二值 (th_mode base/contour, case)
- L7: 软件参考仿真 sim.py 与结果比对 (SoftwareSim/sim.py, PIL transform, PSNR via compare.py)

### Unit 3 (intermediate): 数据驱动基础设施——Generator
- L8: CountGenerator——像素坐标计数器
- L9: RowsGenerator——FIFO行缓存
- L10: WindowGenerator——滑动窗口生成 (shift registers, input_ack handshake)
- L11: FrameController——线性帧缓存寻址 (sequential R/W)
- L12: FrameController2——二维随机寻址 (y*im_width+x, multiplier, for geometry)

### Unit 4 (intermediate): 点运算进阶——定点运算与乘法器
- L13: Graying——RGB转灰度与常系数乘法器
- L14: ContrastTransform与LightnessTransform——定点增益 (multiplier, True2Comp signed)

### Unit 5 (intermediate): 局部滤波器——窗口运算
- L15: MeanFilter——加法树与移位近似除法
- L16: RankFilter——比较矩阵与排序网络 (big_flag, Encoder, median)
- L17: ErosionDilationBin与形态学/模板匹配 (ErosionDilationBin, MatchTemplateBin, ThresholdLocal composition)

### Unit 6 (intermediate): 几何变换
- L18: 流式几何变换——Crop/Mirror/Pan (count-based, range check)
- L19: 随机访问几何变换——Rotate (SinLUT/CosLUT, inverse mapping, FrameController2)
- L20: Scale与Shear——定点缩放与错切

### Unit 7 (intermediate): Connector与InOut——数据流与硬件接口
- L21: Connector——多路选择与数据格式转换 (Mux, DataCombin/Split/Delay/WidthConvert, color format converters)
- L22: InOut——VGA时序、摄像头采集与帧存储 (VGA640x480, camCap, IIC_Ctrl, Bram8x320x240)

### Unit 8 (advanced): 板级系统集成——AXI与Zynq
- L23: BoardInit_AXI——AXI4-Lite寄存器聚合IP (S00_AXI register file, top wrapper, the register map)
- L24: 板上系统数据通路与Block Design (TestOnBoard build.tcl BD, camera→pipeline→VGA dataflow)
- L25: PS端C驱动与寄存器配置 (BoardInit_AXI.h/.c driver, Main.c, Format2Fixed)

### Unit 9 (advanced): 工程化、测试与二次开发
- L26: 测试方法学闭环——从.dat到PSNR (create.py/testbench/convert.py/compare.py full loop, testbench 6-variant pattern)
- L27: IP打包与Com2DocHDL文档生成 (component.xml, xgui tcl/gtcl, ::description→GUI)
- L28: 二次开发——新增一个图像处理IP (how to add a new IP following the conventions)

That's 28 lectures across 9 units. That fits "中型项目 15-30篇" nicely (actually it's a fairly large project given the breadth, so 28 is appropriate). Let me reconsider — maybe I can tighten. Actually 28 is good for this breadth. But let me reconsider whether some can merge. The planner says don't force-split. 28 lectures for a library with 40+ IPs across 8 categories plus full system integration is reasonable.

52	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
53	
54	module ContrastTransform(
55		clk,
56		rst_n,
57		ct_scale,
58		in_enable,
59		in_data,
60		out_ready,
61		out_data
62	    );
63	
64		/*
65		::description
66		This module's working mode.
67		::range
68		0 for Pipelines, 1 for Req-ack
69		*/
70		parameter[0 : 0] work_mode = 0; 
71		/*
72		::description
73		Channels for color, 1 for gray, 3 for rgb, etc.
74		*/
75		parameter color_channels = 3;
76		/*
77		::description
78		Color's bit wide
79		::range
80		1 - 12
81		*/
82		parameter[3: 0] color_width = 8;
83		/*
84		::description
85		Delay for multiplier.
86		::range
87		Depend your multilpliers' configurations
88		*/
89		parameter mul_delay = 3;
90		/*
91		::description
92		Clock.
93		*/
94		input clk;
95		/*
96		::description
97		Reset, active low.
98		*/
99		input rst_n;
100		/*
101		::description
102		Scale for contrast, fixed, 12bits.12bits.
103		*/
104		input[23 : 0] ct_scale;
105		/*
106		::description
107		Input data enable, in pipelines mode, it works as another rst_n, in req-ack mode, only it is high will in_data can be changes.
108		*/
109		input in_enable;
110		/*
111		::description
112		Input data, it must be synchronous with in_enable.
113		*/
114		input [color_channels * color_width - 1 : 0] in_data;
115		/*
116		::description
117		Output data ready, in both two mode, it will be high while the out_data can be read.
118		*/
119		output out_ready;
120		/*
121		::description
122		Output data, it will be synchronous with out_ready.
123		*/
124		output[color_channels * color_width - 1 : 0] out_data;
125	
126		reg[2 : 0] con_enable;
127	
128		genvar i;
129		generate
130	
131			always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n or negedge in_enable) begin
132				if(~rst_n || ~in_enable)
133					con_enable <= 0;
134				else if(con_enable == mul_delay + 1)
135					con_enable <= con_enable;
136				else
137					con_enable <= con_enable +1;
138			end
139			assign out_ready = con_enable == mul_delay + 1 ? 1 : 0; 
140	
141			`define h (i + 1) * color_width - 1
142			`define l i * color_width
143			for (i = 0; i < color_channels; i = i + 1) begin: channel
144				wire[11 : 0] mul_a;
145				wire[23 : 0] mul_b;
146				wire[23 : 0] mul_p;
147	
148				if(work_mode == 0) begin
149					assign mul_a = in_data[`h : `l];
150					assign mul_b = ct_scale;
151				end else begin 
152					reg[11 : 0] reg_mul_a;
153					reg[23 : 0] reg_mul_b;
154					always @(posedge in_enable) begin
155						reg_mul_a = in_data[`h : `l];
156						reg_mul_b = ct_scale;
157					end
158					assign mul_a = reg_mul_a;
159					assign mul_b = reg_mul_b;
160				end

Now I have confirmed:
- **ContrastTransform**: per-channel `Multiplier12x24CT` (12-bit pixel × 24-bit fixed ct_scale), overflow handling, pipelined. ct_scale is 12.12 fixed-point.
- **Mirror**: mode 2-bit (00 horizontal, 01 vertical, 10/11 all), uses in_count_x/in_count_y, streaming.
- **ErosionDilationBin**: morphology with `template` (window_width² bits), mode 0=Erosion/1=Dilation, uses `pre_now_pix = pix ^ mode | ~template` function and a tree reduction (AND/OR). Binary images.
- **BoardInit_AXI_v1_0.v top**: AXI4-Lite slave wrapper, exposes all parameter output ports (th_mode, th1, th2, ct_scale, lm_gain signed, rank, ed_mode, ed_template, mt_template, crop_top/bottom/left/right, mirror_mode, offset_x/y signed, scale_x/y, sh_u/v signed, angle, sels) + rst_all_n, and instantiates BoardInit_AXI_v1_0_S00_AXI. So the top wrapper connects AXI registers → output ports that drive the PL fabric IPs.
